EDCS-00380087.text_edition: \n Caesia |(mulieris) liberta Phaenusa hic sita est\n
EDCS-00380087.translation: Caesia Phaenusa, freedwoman of a woman, is laid here.
EDCS-00380087.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caesia Phaenusa, a freedwoman, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380089.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription identifying Nysa, a freedwoman, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380092.translation: To Silvinus, who lived 10 years. Betulena Mo[---], mother, and Successa, wife, and Silvina, daughter, made [this] for their well-deserving son. [---] CAMAN[---]. Made for [---] well-deserving, who lived more or less 20 years and 4 months. Crescens made [this] for his well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-00380092.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription likely commemorating Silvinus and a daughter, commissioned by his mother, wife, and sister, with a separate dedication by Crescens for his daughter, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380096.translation: To the divine shades. To Ianuaria Pompeia of Civitas. Primus placed [this] for his cohabitant and for himself.
EDCS-00380096.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Ianuaria Pompeia by Primus, who also set it up for himself, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380097.translation: Quarta, of Tullianus, is laid here.
EDCS-00380097.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription identifying Quarta, related to Tullianus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380100.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription indicating that Avillia Olivola placed the monument, likely funerary, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380101.translation: To Publius Annius Gae, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe. Publius Annius Gavianus, heir of Publius, and Publius Annius Campuleianus, grandson, [made this] for their excellent parent.
EDCS-00380101.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the heir and grandson to Publius Annius Gae, a prominent citizen, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380102.translation: Titus Antonius, son of Caius, duovir by right of jurisdiction [---].
EDCS-00380102.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying Titus Antonius, a duovir, likely a public record or dedication, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380104.translation: Baebia Chreste, freedwoman of Quintus, is laid here.
EDCS-00380104.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Baebia Chreste, a freedwoman, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380111.text_edition: \n Calviae Fortunatae inemitabili sic feminae quae vixit annis LXXXI mensibus III diebus V haec nubsit sic maritum in annis XVI cum quo vixit nnis XVII cui fedem sic exhibuit usque ad diem vitae suae Valerius Gerontius gener una cum nepotibus eius bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-00380111.translation: To Calviae Fortunatae, incomparable woman, who lived 81 years, 3 months, and 5 days. She married and lived with her husband for 16 years, with whom she lived 17 years. Valerius Gerontius, her son-in-law, along with her grandsons, made [this] for the well-deserving [woman] until the day of her death.
EDCS-00380111.summary: This is a funerary inscription detailing the life and family of Calvia Fortunata, commissioned by her son-in-law and grandsons, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380122.translation: To Clement, servant of Caesar (?), Cascia S. erected this for her well-deserving father.
EDCS-00380122.summary: A funerary dedication set up by Cascia S. for her father, possibly named Clement.

EDCS-00380123.translation: Marcus Caecilius Martialis erected this for his well-deserving mother, Caecilia Dorophorus.
EDCS-00380123.summary: A funerary dedication set up by Marcus Caecilius Martialis for his mother.

EDCS-00380124.translation: Cinura rests here. Silo erected this for her.
EDCS-00380124.summary: A funerary dedication set up by Silo for Cinura, possibly a slave or freedwoman of Lucius Salvius.

EDCS-00380125.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Chara, fellow slave of Hexocus, slave of Salvius Capito, well-deserving, for whom he lived 20 years, erected this.
EDCS-00380125.summary: A funerary dedication set up by Hexocus' fellow slave Chara for her.

EDCS-00380127.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Avillia, daughter of Spurius, [---] Proc[---]
EDCS-00380127.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication for Avillia.

EDCS-00380129.translation: Marcus Hos[---] Cornelia [---] Pamphilus (?) [---] from the funds [---]
EDCS-00380129.summary: A fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or funerary monument, mentioning Marcus, Cornelia, and Pamphilus.

EDCS-00380131.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Vibius, son of Lucius, [---] OV[---]
EDCS-00380131.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication for Lucius Vibius.

EDCS-00380134.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Mestrius, evocatus, erected this for his incomparable wife Asinia Stephanis, who lived 30 years and with me 15 years and 5 months. Farewell, Asyncritus.
EDCS-00380134.summary: A funerary dedication by Aurelius Mestrius for his wife Asinia Stephanis, providing details of their married life and an address to Asyncritus.

EDCS-00380136.translation: For Julia Augusta, mother of the Emperor, of the camp, of the Senate, and of the fatherland, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-00380136.summary: A public honorific inscription for Julia Augusta, mother of an emperor, decreed by the local council.

EDCS-00380149.translation: [---]di Marcus / [---]s Cele/[---] erected for the well-deserving. // Pauludirisi biba[---]
EDCS-00380149.summary: A fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary monument, mentioning Marcus and possibly a name Cele[---].

EDCS-00380150.translation: Chrestus, freedman of the Emperor, made this for Felix, a freedman, who lived for 3 years.
EDCS-00380150.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a freedman of the emperor for another freedman, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380151.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Cleppius Passarion, who lived for 5 years, 2 months, and 29 days. Modestus and Restituta set this up for their most devoted son.
EDCS-00380151.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy named Cleppius Passarion, set up by his parents, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380153.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For [Tyche?]...
EDCS-00380153.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely for a person named Tyche, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380158.translation: ... IIvir. For Gaius Nunnius, son of Gaius, [and?] Aeflania Rufa(?), daughter of Gaius. [and?] Selene, mother...
EDCS-00380158.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a man who held the office of IIvir, and mentions his mother and possibly wife, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380159.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavia Auxesis made this for her excellent and deserving brother, Flavius Proto.
EDCS-00380159.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a sister for her brother, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380160.translation: Lat ia Primigenia made this for Lati Fortunata, her sister, and for Gaius Maetilius Coffius.
EDCS-00380160.summary: This is a funerary or dedication inscription set up by a sister and a man for two individuals, likely Lati Fortunata and possibly another relative or associate, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380161.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator. Marcus Pontius Pudens willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380161.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator by Marcus Pontius Pudens, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380162.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Atimetus [made this] for his deserving freedman Onesimenus.
EDCS-00380162.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a master for his deserving freedman, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380163.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Ceselius Montanus set this up for his deserving son Mario Abuccio, who lived for 25 years.
EDCS-00380163.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a father for his son, who died at the age of 25, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380164.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Saturnina [and] Acilius set this up for their deserving daughter Nummia Equae, who lived for 11 months and 5 days.
EDCS-00380164.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by parents for their young daughter, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-00380165.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Socellius Ursio, to his deserving father.
EDCS-00380165.summary: A funerary dedication from a son to his well-deserving father.

EDCS-00380166.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus memoriae Sabinae animae innocenti dulcissimae Maximus amicus eius bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-00380166.translation: To the spirits of the departed, in memory of Sabina, sweetest innocent soul. Maximus, her friend, made this for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-00380166.summary: A funerary dedication by a friend to a deceased woman, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380167.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Gerontius Felicissimus. To his dearly beloved wife Eventiana, with whom he lived for 17 years and 6 months. He leaves her alone. Erected for her, who deserved well, for 150 denarii.
EDCS-00380167.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband to his wife, recording her age at death and the cost of the monument, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-00380168.translation: For Metilius Apronianus, his deserving father. Apronianus, his son, made this.
EDCS-00380168.summary: A funerary dedication from a son to his father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380169.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Vibius Restuta, his dearest daughter, who lived 25 years, 11 months, and 13 days. Her parents made this for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-00380169.summary: A funerary dedication by parents to their deceased daughter, specifying her age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380170.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Flavia Marina. Titus Flavius [---] [---] who lived 16 years.
EDCS-00380170.summary: A funerary dedication to a young woman, recording her name and age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380171.translation: To our son, who lived 17 years and 5 days. His father and mother, Vibia Sodole, made this for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-00380171.summary: A funerary dedication by parents to their deceased son, specifying his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380172.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Camillus Severus. Marcus Camillius Severus, his father, and Minia Copiola, his mother, made this for their dearest son.
EDCS-00380172.summary: A funerary dedication by parents to their deceased son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380173.translation: For Clunia Modesta, his deserving wife. Tiberius Claudius [---]sonus(?) made this.
EDCS-00380173.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband to his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380174.summary: A simple funerary epitaph indicating burial, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380175.translation: To the departed spirits. To Iulia Felicul(a), Iulius [---] to his wife [---].
EDCS-00380175.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a husband to his deceased wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380178.translation: For the public affairs of the people of Aeclanum, under the supervision of Maian[icus Paulus], Lucius Volusius Adiectus made [this].
EDCS-00380178.summary: This is a dedication related to public works in the town of Aeclanum, possibly built under the supervision of Maianicus Paulus.

EDCS-00380179.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Aurelius Valerius Diocletianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Valerius and Flavius Valerius Constantinus and Galerius Valerius Maximianus, most noble Caesars, [measured] 83 miles from Aufidena and 8 miles from Aequum.
EDCS-00380179.summary: This inscription records the distances from two towns (Aufidena and Aequum) as measured by or during the reign of the emperors Diocletian, Maximian, Constantius, and Galerius, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-02900083.translation: To Paccia Sabina, daughter of Manius. Coelia Tertulla, daughter of Marcus, [dedicated this].
EDCS-02900083.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Paccia Sabina by Coelia Tertulla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-02900091.translation: To Lucius Tilius Trassa, son of Gaius, of the Aemilia tribe, centurion of the [first] legion [---], and to Gaius Tilius, his brother, and to Gaius Tilius, [father], and to Obinia, daughter of Lucius, [mother], and to Castricia, daughter of Lucius, [---], and to Lucius Tilius, son of Lucius, son [---].
EDCS-02900091.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a centurion to his deceased family members, including his brother, father, mother, and son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-02900104.translation: To Quintus Pontius Quadratus, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, duumvir, and to his freedman Quintus Pontius Edistus. Quintus Pontius Liberalis made [this].
EDCS-02900104.summary: This is a dedication by Quintus Pontius Liberalis to a duumvir and his freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-02900106.translation: To Publius Crittius Firmus, son of Publius, of the Cornelia tribe, chief centurion of the XXII legion, tribune of the VI cohort of vigiles, prefect of the camp of the XII Fulminata legion, duumvir with judicial power, priest of the deified Augustus. Crittia Priscilla, daughter of Publius, of the Cornelia tribe, priestess of Agrippina Augusta. Publius Crittius Firmus, son of Publius, of the Cornelia tribe, his son [dedicated this].
EDCS-02900106.summary: This inscription honors a distinguished Roman military and civic official and his family, detailing his extensive career and priesthoods, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-02900107.translation: To the departed spirits. To Bi[---]riae Soteriae. Iulius Clymaenus made [this] for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-02900107.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a husband to his deceased wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-02900108.translation: To the departed spirits. To Gaius Eggius Rufinus, who lived XXXXV years. Gaius Eggeius Primulus made [this] for his well-deserving brother.
EDCS-02900108.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a brother to his deceased brother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-02900109.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Eppius Firmianus made this for his well-deserving freedman, Gaius Eppius Acindynus.
EDCS-02900109.summary: A funerary monument erected by a patron for his freedman, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-02900110.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Rufinius Successus made this for his pupil, Rufinius Silbinus.
EDCS-02900110.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a teacher to his student, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-02900111.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Naevius Eutyches made this for his wife, Sertoria Villana.
EDCS-02900111.summary: A funerary monument erected by a husband for his wife, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-02900113.translation: By public decree of the decurions. To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, conqueror of Armenia, greatest conqueror of Parthia, Mede, conqueror of Germany, of Sarmatia, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 29th time, commander for the 8th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, son of the divine Antoninus, brother of the divine Verus, greatest conqueror of Parthia, grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-grandson of the divine Trajan, conqueror of Parthia, great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva.
EDCS-02900113.summary: A dedicatory inscription to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, listing his numerous titles and honors, likely dating to the period of his reign (161-180 CE).

EDCS-02900116.translation: Marcus Mindius Marcellus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, arranged for the repair of the wall, tower, and half-tower, by decree of the senate.
EDCS-02900116.summary: An inscription recording the repair of public works (wall, towers) by a magistrate, likely dating to the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-02900117.translation: Lucius Fulvius Soteric, physician, made this while alive for himself and for his patron Lucius Fulvius Herophilus, Augustalis physician, and for his patroness Fulvia Prota, freedwoman of Lucius, and for Aoedimus Fulvius, and for his mother Aucta Fulvia, and for Argenus Fulvius, and for his mother Zopyra, and for Babbia Vitalis, and for Lucius Fulvius, son of Lucius, [---], and for Fulvia, daughter of Lucius, [---], and for Fulvia [---].
EDCS-02900117.summary: A tomb inscription commissioned by a physician, listing himself, his family members, patrons, and possibly slaves or freedmen, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-02900118.translation: Numerius Benius Rufio, son of Marcus.
EDCS-02900118.summary: A simple funerary inscription identifying the deceased, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-02900120.translation: To Gavoleia Rufa, daughter of Publius, and to Quintus Seppius Rufus, son of Quintus, according to the will and by the arbitration of Crittia Polla, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-02900120.summary: An inscription related to the execution of a will, mentioning beneficiaries and an arbiter, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-02900121.translation: To the genius of the Aequi Tutici neighborhood. Seppia.
EDCS-02900121.summary: A dedication to the local genius of a neighborhood by an individual, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-02900125.translation: Dedicated on the 5th day before the Kalends of January, when Saturninus and Gallus were consuls. Sacred to Juno Regina, by order of Petilia Irena, daughter of Gaius. Gavia Auxime, mother, [gave] the place by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-02900125.summary: A religious dedication to Juno Regina, specifying the date by consuls and indicating the land was granted by decree of the local council, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-02900126.translation: To the departed spirits. The fellow members of the Collegium Augustianum erected this for their well-deserving colleague Lucius Catilius Primus, at their own expense.
EDCS-02900126.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a collegium (association) for a deceased member, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-02900127.translation: To the departed spirits. The members of the collegium erected this for Gaius Marcius Hilarus, at their own expense.
EDCS-02900127.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a collegium for a deceased member, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-02900128.translation: To the departed spirits. Vibius Aldius Cil(o) and his mother Metatiria(?) erected this for their well-deserving relatives Anius Ap(p)ius Verecundus Lilius and possibly others.
EDCS-02900128.summary: This is a funerary dedication by family members for deceased relatives, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-02900130.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Monius(?) erected this for his dearest son Titus Monius, son of Marcus, who lived 3 years, 11 months, and 9 days.
EDCS-02900130.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child, erected by his father, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-02900131.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Aemilius M[a]m[us erected this for his] mother Oppia Maximilla, who lived 47 years and 30 days.
EDCS-02900131.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by a son for his mother, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-03000109.translation: Quintus Herennius Aequos, son of Gaius, of the Claudia tribe, twice aedile, and four times a duovir, for a second time also quinquennial duovir, for himself and Corneli[a ---] erected [this].
EDCS-03000109.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a local magistrate and his wife, likely dating to the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-03000113.translation: For Sextus Peducaeus Iucundus, the Augustalis, for Sextus Peducaeus Hector, and for Peducaea Ephire.
EDCS-03000113.summary: This is a funerary dedication likely for family members, one of whom held the prestigious position of Augustalis, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-03000114.translation: For Muttiena Silvana(?), daughter of Publius, [and for ---] Lucconia Primi[3 ---] daughter.
EDCS-03000114.summary: This is a funerary dedication for two women, likely related, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-03000119.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Lucerinus Homoeus erected this for his well-deserving wife Appuleia Soteris.
EDCS-03000119.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by a husband for his wife, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-03000120.translation: Publius Caelius Felix, freedman of Publius, a *sagarus* (priest of a foreign cult?) and Augustalis, for himself and Caellia Danae and his own people, 16 feet in front and 14 feet in depth.
EDCS-03000120.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a freedman who held religious office, specifying the dimensions of his burial plot, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-03000122.translation: Gaius Alfius Verna, freedman of Gaius, made this for himself while alive; here he lies.
EDCS-03000122.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Alfius Verna, who set up the monument for himself.

EDCS-03000123.translation: May this journey be fortunate for you; pause, traveler, and briefly understand my fate. My name was Propasis; I was snatched from the bloom of youth by a harsh death. For I had completed eight years of age when my life was lost. Grieving parents mourn; you could complain much if delay were welcome. Pollia Helena, freedwoman of Marcus.
EDCS-03000123.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Pollia Helena, who died at the age of eight, with a lament from her parents.

EDCS-03000125.translation: Iustus and Sabinus for their most unfortunate parents. Carmhenius Niger (?) donated.
EDCS-03000125.summary: This inscription records a donation made by Carmhenius Niger (?) for his parents, Iustus and Sabinus.

EDCS-03000126.translation: To Magullius, son of Numerius, of the Cornelia tribe, Flaccus, priest, aedile, and duovir. By his will and at the discretion of his wife, Patulacia Polla, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-03000126.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription detailing the public career of Magullius Flaccus, dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-03000127.translation: To our lord Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maxsentius Invictus Augustus, consul, with tribunician power, from Aufidena, 77.
EDCS-03000127.summary: This is an inscription dedicating something to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maxsentius Invictus, likely erected at Aufidena.

EDCS-03000128.translation: She made this for herself and her family while alive. Tifas, patron; Dama, husband.
EDCS-03000128.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where a woman, Tifas, made the monument for herself and her family, mentioning a patron and her husband Dama.

EDCS-03300047.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Secunda, slave of Apronianus, lived 55 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-03300047.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Secunda, a slave of Apronianus, who lived 55 years.

EDCS-03300048.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Servilius Romulus.

EDCS-03300069.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Publius Gerellanus Agapetus lived 65 years.
EDCS-03300069.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Gerellanus Agapetus, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-03300072.translation: Here lie the ashes and bones of Polycratis, who undeservedly suffered a miserable death. O Horcus, how cruel were the fates that gave me death! May you, my fellow citizens, live on in happy years, and may I...
EDCS-03300072.summary: This is a funerary epitaph lamenting the death of Polycratis and invoking the underworld god Horcus and the Fates.

EDCS-03300074.translation: To the emperors Caesar Gaius Aurelius Valerius Diocletian Pius Felix Invictus Augustus and Caesar Marcus Valerius Maximian Pius Felix...
EDCS-03300074.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription likely honoring the emperors Diocletian and Maximian, possibly from an official monument.

EDCS-03700093.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Arthemisus Brutti Praesentis, slave and supervisor, set up (this monument) for Felicia Brutti Praesentis, his fellow slave, who deserved well.
EDCS-03700093.summary: A funerary inscription set up by a slave for his fellow slave, dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-03700094.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Amar(a)ntus set up (this monument) for Callimedon, slave of Betitius Pius, his father, who deserved well.
EDCS-03700094.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Amar(a)ntus for his father Callimedon, who was a slave, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-03700095.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Seppius Silvanus, a tenant farmer of the Lucilian estate, set up (this monument) for his fellow slave Seppia Esperis, who deserved well.
EDCS-03700095.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Seppius Silvanus for his fellow slave Seppia Esperis, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-03700097.translation: Marcus Obinius [---] is buried here [---] [---]ano Ob[---]
EDCS-03700097.summary: A funerary inscription fragment that records the burial of a person named Marcus Obinius.

EDCS-04900042.translation: Sacred to the August Lares. Quintus Rastic[a]nus Varus, Gaius Pontidius Amerimnus, Aulus Allienus Primus, Marcus Vibius Achoristus, the first quinquennial Augustales, made (this) at their own expense.
EDCS-04900042.summary: An inscription recording the dedication of a sacred space or object to the August Lares by a group of Augustales, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-04900043.summary: A very brief inscription identifying Vibia Agatha as the freedwoman of Publius, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-04900044.summary: A fragmentary inscription that appears to record a dedication to someone named Aphrodisia and mentions a son.

EDCS-04900046.summary: A very fragmented inscription that seems to refer to a freedwoman and possibly a dedication or gift.

EDCS-05000129.translation: To Coelia Tertulla, daughter of Marcus, priestess of the divine Augusta. Gabbia Tertulla, daughter of Marcus, her grandmother, erected this; it was decreed by the town council that she, the first in the municipality at the age of seven, be granted the priesthood.
EDCS-05000129.summary: This is a funerary monument or dedication honoring a young girl who was appointed to a priesthood by a local council.

EDCS-05000130.translation: To Antonius Iustinianus, governor, defender, and judge of all sins. The council and people of Larinum [dedicate this] to their patron.
EDCS-05000130.summary: This is a dedication from the town of Larinum to a patron named Antonius Iustinianus, likely an official or benefactor.

EDCS-05000131.translation: To the departed spirits. To Lollia, her most devoted housemate, with whom she lived for 22 years.
EDCS-05000131.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lollia, recording her name, relationship to the deceased, and length of cohabitation.

EDCS-05000132.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar, son of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus, father of the fatherland. The colony Vibina [dedicates this].
EDCS-05000132.summary: This is a dedication from the colony Vibina to Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar, likely honoring his imperial status.

EDCS-05000133.translation: To Gaius Lollus Benivolus, freedman of Gaius, magistrate, Augustalis, and quinquennalis. His heirs, by his will, erected this; Pontia Deutera, his freedwoman wife, having been placed in the same location before.
EDCS-05000133.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Lollus Benivolus, an Augustalis magistrate, erected by his heirs and mentioning his wife.

EDCS-05200076.text_edition: \n Caius Vibius Octavius Minervae dono dedit\n
EDCS-05200076.translation: Gaius Vibius Octavius dedicated this gift to Minerva.
EDCS-05200076.summary: This is a dedication of a gift to the goddess Minerva by Gaius Vibius Octavius.

EDCS-05200083.text_edition: \n Verzo Themi filius de liburna Tritone militavit annos XX vixit nnos XXXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-05200083.translation: Verzo, son of Them, served in the liburna Triton for 20 years, lived 40 years, is buried here.
EDCS-05200083.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Verzo, son of Them, recording his military service and lifespan.

EDCS-05200085.text_edition: \n AT bene merenti et Telesfhoro sic parenti Cornelius Niceta\n
EDCS-05200085.translation: Cornelius Niceta [dedicated this] to the well-deserving AT [and] to Telesphorus, his father.
EDCS-05200085.summary: This is a dedication by Cornelius Niceta to two individuals, AT and Telesphorus, who is identified as his father.

EDCS-06100148.text_edition: \n Flavia Recepta sacerdos Matris Magnae et Syriae deae ex imperio fecit loco dato decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-06100148.translation: Flavia Recepta, priestess of the Mother Goddess and the goddess Syria, made this at her own expense in a place granted by decree of the town council.
EDCS-06100148.summary: This is a dedication of a religious structure or object by Flavia Recepta, a priestess, funded by her and approved by local authorities.

EDCS-06100149.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Silbana sic sibi viba sic posuit vixit annos \n
EDCS-06100149.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelia Silbana dedicated this for herself and her family; she lived [X] years.
EDCS-06100149.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Silbana, recording her dedication for herself and her family and her lifespan.

EDCS-06100150.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Silvana vixit annos hic sita est Aurelius Felicissimus patronae suae bene merenti\n
EDCS-06100150.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Silvana lived [number] years; she lies here. Aurelius Felicissimus [dedicated this] to his deserving patroness.
EDCS-06100150.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a freedman to his patroness, Aurelia Silvana, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-06100151.text_edition: \n Marcus Sabbius Maximus vixit annos X menses X hic situs\n
EDCS-06100151.translation: Marcus Sabbius Maximus lived 10 years and 10 months; he lies here.
EDCS-06100151.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the short life of Marcus Sabbius Maximus, likely a child.

EDCS-06100153.text_edition: \n Cresces Euplunis threptus vixit annos XV hic situs est\n
EDCS-06100153.translation: Cresces, slave of Euplis, lived 15 years; he lies here.
EDCS-06100153.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cresces, a slave, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-06100154.text_edition: \n Marcus Larcius Felicio vixit annos XXXX\n
EDCS-06100154.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age at death of Marcus Larcius Felicio.

EDCS-06100155.text_edition: \n Magia Zosima vixit annos III hic sita est\n
EDCS-06100155.translation: Magia Zosima lived 3 years; she lies here.
EDCS-06100155.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the young age of death for Magia Zosima.

EDCS-07000293.text_edition: \n Lucanius Ceianus IIIIvir quinquennalis vixit an[nos ------\n
EDCS-07000293.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucanius Ceianus, an official holding a quinquennial office, likely from an Italian town.

EDCS-07000294.text_edition: \n Faustus Caesaris Fausto filio suo fecit et sibi\n
EDCS-07000294.translation: Faustus made [this] for Faustus, his son, and for himself.
EDCS-07000294.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Faustus for his son Faustus and for himself, suggesting a family memorial.

EDCS-07000302.text_edition: \n Caius Baebius Cai filius Claudia Hispo IIIIvir aedilis IIIIvir iure dicundo augur quinquennalis censoria potestate\n
EDCS-07000302.translation: Gaius Baebius, son of Caius, son of Claudia Hispo, *quattuorvir aedilis*, *quattuorvir iure dicundo*, *augur quinquennalis*, holding censorial power.
EDCS-07000302.summary: An inscription recording the extensive career of a local magistrate, Caius Baebius, likely from a Roman colony.

EDCS-07000303.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fabiae Quinti filiae Stratonice optimae ac piissimae coniugi Lucius Plutius Hermes\n
EDCS-07000303.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Fabia Stratonice, most excellent and devoted wife, daughter of Quintus. Lucius Plutius Hermes [dedicated this].
EDCS-07000303.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a husband, Lucius Plutius Hermes, for his deceased wife, Fabia Stratonice.

EDCS-07000304.text_edition: \n Antonia Tyche vixit annos L hic sita est\n
EDCS-07000304.translation: Antonia Tyche lived 50 years; she lies here.
EDCS-07000304.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the age at death of Antonia Tyche.

EDCS-07000305.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Apicula.

EDCS-07000306.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Anacleius.

EDCS-07000308.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerius Spes vixit annos hic situs est\n
EDCS-07000308.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valerius Spes lived (and) is buried here.
EDCS-07000308.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valerius Spes, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07000309.text_edition: \n Phileros Claudi Thesmi servus vixit annos XXXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-07000309.translation: Phileros, slave of Claudius Thesmius, lived for 40 years (and) is buried here.
EDCS-07000309.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Phileros, a slave of Claudius Thesmius.

EDCS-07000312.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Sperata.

EDCS-07000313.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Attia Anthusa, a freedwoman.

EDCS-07000315.text_edition: \n Fadius Cominus vixit annos XXXX ollam eius si quis violavit ad inferos non recipiatur\n
EDCS-07000315.translation: Fadius Cominus lived for 40 years. If anyone violates his jar, may he not be received among the departed.
EDCS-07000315.summary: This is a funerary epitaph with a curse for disturbing the grave of Fadius Cominus.

EDCS-07000316.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Marcius Cosmus Augustalis vixit annos LX hic situs est\n
EDCS-07000316.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Marcius Cosmus Augustalis lived for 60 years (and) is buried here.
EDCS-07000316.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Marcius Cosmus Augustalis, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-07000317.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tranquillus SEPICY filius servus vixit annos X pater filio\n
EDCS-07000317.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Tranquillus, son of Sepicy, slave, lived for 10 years. (Erected by) his father for his son.
EDCS-07000317.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tranquillus, a slave, erected by his father.

EDCS-07000318.text_edition: \n Felix Stocasmi vixit annos III menses V\n
EDCS-07000318.translation: Felix Stocasmi lived for 3 years and 5 months.
EDCS-07000318.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Felix Stocasmi.

EDCS-07000319.text_edition: \n Publius Valerius Epagathus vixit annos LXXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-07000319.translation: Publius Valerius Epagathus lived 75 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-07000319.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Valerius Epagathus, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-07000321.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Caelius Diogenes vixit annos XXXV Pietas filio\n
EDCS-07000321.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Caelius Diogenes lived 35 years. Pietas [erected this] for her son.
EDCS-07000321.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Pietas for her son, Lucius Caelius Diogenes, noting his lifespan.

EDCS-07000323.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Divi filio Augusto pontifici maximo patri patriae\n
EDCS-07000323.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the deified [Augustus], pontifex maximus, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-07000323.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Caesar Augustus, listing his titles and divine connections.

EDCS-07000324.text_edition: \n Germanico Caesari Tiberi Augusti filio Divi Augusti nepoti\n
EDCS-07000324.translation: To Germanicus Caesar, son of Tiberius Augustus, grandson of the deified Augustus.
EDCS-07000324.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Germanicus Caesar, noting his familial ties to the Julio-Claudian emperors.

EDCS-07000326.text_edition: \n Tullius Luci filius Caesius Marci filius IIIIviri quinquennales faciendum curaverunt eidemque probaverunt\n
EDCS-07000326.translation: Tullius, son of Lucius, Caesius, son of Marcus, the quinquennial duovirs, arranged for its construction and approved it for the same [purpose/person].
EDCS-07000326.summary: This inscription records the actions of two quinquennial duovirs, Tullius Caesius and his colleague, regarding the construction and approval of a public work.

EDCS-07000331.text_edition: \n Caio Mario Cai filio Stellatina legione XX Siciliana Faustus libertus in fronte pedes XVI in agro pedes XVI\n
EDCS-07000331.translation: To Gaius Marius, son of Caius, of the Stellatina tribe, [a soldier] of the 20th Legion Siciliana. Faustus, his freedman, [granted/provided] 16 feet in front and 16 feet in the field.
EDCS-07000331.summary: This is a dedication, likely for a tomb or plot of land, by the freedman Faustus for Gaius Marius, a soldier in the 20th Legion, specifying dimensions.

EDCS-07000333.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Cosinio Cai- Su Cosini\n
EDCS-07000333.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Marcus Cosinius, son of Caius. [Belonging to] Su[vius?], son of Cosinius.
EDCS-07000333.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning Marcus Cosinius and possibly another individual, indicating a dedication to the underworld spirits.

EDCS-07000334.text_edition: \n Marco Albeio Marci filio Stellatina Flacco Tofariae Marci filiae Iustae Caius Albeius Marci filius Stellatina filius heres fecit\n
EDCS-07000334.translation: To Marcus Albeius Flaccus, son of Marcus, of the Stellatina tribe, and to Iusta, daughter of Marcus, of Tofariae. Gaius Albeius, son of Marcus, of the Stellatina tribe, son and heir, made [this].
EDCS-07000334.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Gaius Albeius for his parents, Marcus Albeius Flaccus and Iusta.

EDCS-07400198.text_edition: \n Manio Cornelio |(mulieris) liberto Reparato Manio Cornelio Mani filio Augurino Campaniae Decimi libertae Saturninae suisque\n
EDCS-07400198.translation: To Manius Cornelius Reparatus, freedman of a woman; to Manius Cornelius Augurinus, son of Manius; and to his own [family members?], including Saturnina, freedwoman of Decimus of Campania.
EDCS-07400198.summary: This inscription records a dedication for several individuals, including freedmen and a member of the Cornelius family, likely for a tomb or family monument.

EDCS-07400202.text_edition: \n Aulo Ditonio Auli liberto Eroti A\n
EDCS-07400202.translation: To Aulus Ditonius Eros, freedman of Aulus.
EDCS-07400202.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Aulus Ditonius Eros, a freedman, likely for a tomb or monument.

EDCS-07400203.text_edition: \n Firmilia Cai liberta Cleopatra sita Firmilia |(mulieris) liberta Parata\n
EDCS-07400203.translation: Cleopatra, freedwoman of Firmilia, is buried here, (also) Parata, freedwoman of Firmilia (a woman).
EDCS-07400203.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two freedwomen, Cleopatra and Parata, who were associated with Firmilia.

EDCS-07400207.text_edition: \n Gavia Luci liberta Du sibi et Lucio Gavio Luci liberto Gaviae Luci libertae Gaviae Luci libertae \n
EDCS-07400207.translation: Gavia, freedwoman of Lucius, to herself and to Lucius Gavius, freedman of Lucius, and to Gavia, freedwoman of Lucius, and to Gavia, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-07400207.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Gavia, a freedwoman, for herself and several other freedpersons, likely family members, with common patron Lucius Gavius.

EDCS-07400210.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Maximillae uxori bene merenti Dionysius coniux fecit\n
EDCS-07400210.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Dionysius made this for his deserving wife, Maximilla.
EDCS-07400210.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a husband, Dionysius, for his well-deserving wife, Maximilla.

EDCS-07400219.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tryphenae conservae Parthienio sic <f=E>ecit Parthenio <f=E>ecit \n
EDCS-07400219.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Parthienius made this for Tryphaena, his fellow slave, (and) Parthenius made this.
EDCS-07400219.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Parthienius for his fellow slave Tryphaena, with Parthenius also mentioned, likely the same person.

EDCS-07400234.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Babulliae Severae vixit annos XIX menses III dies XXV Quintus Fufius Helius et Titus Lucceius Proculus et Babullia Cypare filiae dulcissimae et sibi fecerunt\n
EDCS-07400234.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Babullia Severa lived 19 years, 3 months, 25 days. Quintus Fufius Helius and Titus Lucceius Proculus and Babullia Cypare made this for their most beloved daughter and for themselves.
EDCS-07400234.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Babullia Severa, who died at the age of 19, and was dedicated by her parents and another individual.

EDCS-07600155.translation: To the departed spirits of Lucius Abuccius Salvius.
EDCS-07600155.summary: A simple funerary dedication to Lucius Abuccius Salvius.

EDCS-07600159.text_edition: \n Avilliae Ampliatae et Numerio Caesio Frequenti Mariae Luci libertae Thallusae et Galbiae Fortunae Caio Galbio mulieris lib(erto) Trophimo Lucius Marius Luci libertus Euaristus fecit sibi suisque\n
EDCS-07600159.translation: Lucius Marius Euaristus, freedman of Lucius, made this for Avillia Ampliata, and Numerius Caesius Frequens, and Maria Thallusa, freedwoman of Lucius, and Galbia Fortunata, and for Caio Galbio Trophimus, freedman of a woman. (It is dedicated) for himself and his own.
EDCS-07600159.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Lucius Marius Euaristus, a freedman, for himself, his family, and several other individuals including Avillia Ampliata and Numerius Caesius Frequens.

EDCS-07600161.text_edition: \n Publius Chornus Publi filius Oufentina Libo situs\n
EDCS-07600161.translation: Publius Chornus Libo, son of Publius, is buried here.
EDCS-07600161.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying the burial place of Publius Chornus Libo.

EDCS-07600163.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cinnamidi Augusti nostri servae vixit annis XXX Compsinus coniugi bene merenti posuit cum qua vixit annos XVI menses dies sine querella\n
EDCS-07600163.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Compsinus placed this for Cinnamis, a slave-woman of our Augustus, whom he lived with for 16 years, (for his) deserving wife, (with whom he lived) years, months, days, without complaint.
EDCS-07600163.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Compsinus to his wife Cinnamis, a slave-woman, noting their 16 years of marriage without complaint.

EDCS-08200705.translation: Marcus Fadius Valerianus, deceased. Marcus Fadius Valerianus (father) and Minia Valeriana (mother) (dedicated this). Place granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08200705.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the parents of Marcus Fadius Valerianus, with the burial plot provisioned by a decree of the local council.

EDCS-08200707.translation: To the departed spirits. Decimus Vibuleius Fuscus, son of Decimus, of the Fabian tribe, a decurion, lived 60(?) years, 11 months, 16 days. He rests here. Pomponia Hermione, his mother, for her most devoted son.
EDCS-08200707.summary: A funerary epitaph for Decimus Vibuleius Fuscus, a decurion, set up by his mother.

EDCS-08200708.translation: To the departed spirits. Musicus, slave of Maximinus, lived 7 years. He rests here. Leuvius and Cale set this up for their most devoted son, well deserving.
EDCS-08200708.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young slave boy named Musicus, set up by his parents.

EDCS-08200709.translation: In the consulship of Antonius Marcellinus and Petronius Probinus, on the second day before the Nones of May, with the people of Empuria Naunae acclaiming, a bronze tablet, engraved, to be offered to the god of Naunae, for Marcus Salvius Balerius, a most distinguished man, to whom for a long time, according to his wishes and the will of the same people, the honour of patronage has been offered; and they resolved that as for what it would please to do about it, they decreed as follows: Since the devoted people have long ago offered the honour of patronage to Marcus Salvius Balerius publicly, and because he has always conferred immense benefits not only on the townsfolk but also on us ourselves, and has always so loved our Empurium that wherever need arose he provided safety and defence; for which reason it is necessary that he be rewarded. Therefore, it is agreed by the entire people of Empuria Naunitani to offer him an engraved bronze tablet, so that he may worthily receive the honour of patronage conferred upon him by the most devoted people of our Empurium with a willing spirit. Decreed by the praetors Gaius Iulius Secundus and Gaius Idius Memmius, and Gaius Geminius Afrodisius and Gaius Propinquius Aliorius.
EDCS-08200709.summary: A decree from Empuria Naunae recording the public acclamation and offering of a bronze tablet to Marcus Salvius Balerius, honoring his patronage and benefits to the city and its people, likely in the late 1st or early 2nd century CE based on the consuls.

EDCS-08200710.translation: To the departed spirits. Claudia Atticilla, daughter of Tiberius, lived 1 year, 2 months. She rests here.
EDCS-08200710.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young child, Claudia Atticilla, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08200711.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucilia Chrysis lived 35 years. She rests here.
EDCS-08200711.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucilia Chrysis, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08200712.translation: To the departed spirits. Pomponius Euticio lived 22 years. He rests here. For...
EDCS-08200712.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pomponius Euticio, dating to the Roman imperial period, with an incomplete dedication.

EDCS-08200714.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, with tribunician power for the 16th time, consul for the 3rd time, son of the divine Antoninus, grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08200714.summary: A dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, likely on a public monument, recording his titles and dignities, dated to 163 CE.

EDCS-08200715.translation: To Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul for the 2nd time, son of the divine Antoninus, grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08200715.summary: A dedication to Emperor Lucius Aurelius Verus, likely on a public monument, recording his titles and dignities, dated to 162 CE.

EDCS-08200716.translation: Titus Memmius Cinyps Tiberinus gave this as a gift to Isis and Sarapis.
EDCS-08200716.summary: A dedication of a votive offering to the Egyptian deities Isis and Sarapis by Titus Memmius Cinyps Tiberinus.

EDCS-08200718.translation: To the priests. For Curtia Secunda, daughter of Publius, lived 39 years. She rests here. Acilia Cypris, her most unhappy mother, took care [of this].
EDCS-08200718.summary: A funerary epitaph for Curtia Secunda, set up by her grieving mother, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08200719.translation: Gaius Julius Irenaeus, master of the Augustales, dedicated this gift.
EDCS-08200719.summary: This is a dedication by Gaius Julius Irenaeus, who held the position of magister Augustalis.

EDCS-08200720.translation: Otacilia, daughter of Marcus, Secundilla, [donated?] the amphitheater.
EDCS-08200720.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to record a donation by Otacilia Secundilla, possibly of an amphitheater.

EDCS-08200722.translation: Marcus Tuccius Augazo, for his most excellent and devoted son, Marcus Tuccius Cerialis, decorated with the equestrian order by the most sacred Emperor Hadrian Augustus, patron of the municipality, having served as a four-man magistrate, aedile, and also aedile in Brundisium, promised 80,000 sesterces to the municipalities in his memory, so that from its income, on the birthday of his son, every year in the name of a distribution of meat, 20 sesterces will be distributed to each decurion, 12 sesterces to the Augustales, and 10 sesterces to the Mercuriales, and also 8 sesterces per person to the people. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08200722.summary: This is a funerary and endowment inscription by Marcus Tuccius Augazo, commemorating his son Marcus Tuccius Cerialis, and establishing an endowment for public distributions of money and food.

EDCS-08200723.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Publius Tutorius Hermatianus, son of Publius, of the Camilia tribe, lived 12 years. And Aelia Thetis, who lived 37 years. They are buried here. Publius Tutorius Hilarianus for his most beloved son and his most devoted wife.
EDCS-08200723.summary: This is a tombstone for Publius Tutorius Hermatianus and Aelia Thetis, set up by Publius Tutorius Hilarianus.

EDCS-08200724.text_edition: \n Marco Avonio Ampliato Iucundae Pisiniae Lepidae contubernali Atiaticae libertae Avonia Fusca mater posuit\n
EDCS-08200724.translation: Avonia Fusca, mother, placed [this] for her companion Marcus Avronius Ampliatus and Iucunda Pisinna Lepida, freedwoman of Atiatica.
EDCS-08200724.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Avonia Fusca for her companion Marcus Avronius Ampliatus and Iucunda Pisinna Lepida.

EDCS-08200725.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Avidius Rufus, son of Gaius, of the Camilia tribe, lived 55 years.
EDCS-08200725.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Avidius Rufus.

EDCS-08200726.translation: Caius Hoscius Aquila lived 85 years. Peta and Aquila made [this] for their well-deserving father.
EDCS-08200726.summary: This tombstone was erected by Peta and Aquila for their father, Caius Hoscius Aquila.

EDCS-08200727.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Julius Tranquillus, son of Gaius, lived 75 years. And Terraea Agapomenes, his wife, lived 65 years. The funeral was provided for at public expense.
EDCS-08200727.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Julius Tranquillus and his wife Terraea Agapomenes, with the funeral expenses covered by the state.

EDCS-08200730.translation: Lupula lived 80 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08200730.summary: This is a tombstone for Lupula.

EDCS-08200733.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Divi filio Augusto pontifici maximo patri patriae\n
EDCS-08200733.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the Divine [Augustus], chief priest, father of the country.
EDCS-08200733.summary: This is an honorific inscription or titulature for Emperor Augustus.

EDCS-08200735.translation: To Trajan Augustus, conqueror of Germany and Dacia, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 11th time, imperator for the 6th time, consul for the 5th time, father of his country. C. Fulvius Epitynchanus, freedman of Gaius Fulvius, by decree of the decurions, for the honour of the Augustales.
EDCS-08200735.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Trajan by a freedman holding the office of Augustalis, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08200736.translation: To Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, son of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 14th time, imperator for the 6th time, consul for the 5th time, father of his country. The decurions and townspeople of Brundisium.
EDCS-08200736.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Trajan by the local officials and citizens of Brundisium, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08200737.translation: For the health of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius [---]
EDCS-08200737.summary: This is a dedication for the well-being of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08200738.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Gaius Laecanius Bassus Caecina Flaccus, son of Gaius, of the Sabatina tribe, a IIIvir of the mint, who lived 18 years.
EDCS-08200738.summary: This is a tombstone for a young man named Gaius Laecanius Bassus Caecina Flaccus, who held a financial office, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-08200739.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintus Betuius sic Quinti filius Felix speculator centuria Aemili militat sic annos VIII vixit annos XXVI hic situs loco publico\n
EDCS-08200739.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Betuius Felix, son of Quintus, a speculator in the centuria of Aemilius, served for 8 years and lived for 26 years. He is buried here in public ground.
EDCS-08200739.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Betuius Felix, a soldier who served in the centuria of Aemilius, likely from the Roman army during the early to mid-Imperial period.

EDCS-08200740.translation: Iulia Cleopatra, also known as Lesbia, daughter of Gaius Julius Menoetis of Antioch in Syria near Daphne, wife of Malchion, captain of the trireme Triptolemus.
EDCS-08200740.summary: This is an epitaph for Iulia Cleopatra, an Antiochian woman who was the wife of a naval officer, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08200741.translation: Scaeva Liccai, sailor from the liburna Triton, in the centuria of Marcus Vettius, lived 35 years, served [3] years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08200741.summary: This is a tombstone for Scaeva Liccai, a sailor on the liburna Triton, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08200742.translation: [---]ilo, son of Pinthsi, from the quadriga vessel, lived 35 years. He is buried here. Symphoniacus.
EDCS-08200742.summary: This is a tombstone for a man named [---]ilo, possibly a sailor or crew member from a ship called Quadriga, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08200743.translation: Gaius Antonius Achaicus, son of Marcus, of the Maecia tribe, a duumvir with curule power, duumvir with judicial authority, duumvir quinquennalis with censorial power, lived 61 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08200743.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Antonius Achaicus, a prominent local magistrate who held multiple offices, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200744.translation: Tiberius Aulus Aper, a duumvir with curule power, duumvir with judicial authority, lived 53 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08200744.summary: This is a tombstone for Tiberius Aulus Aper, a local magistrate, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200745.translation: To Tiberius Aulius Marinus, son of Tiberius, of the Maecia tribe, who held the office of quaestor four times with aedilician power and four times as a magistrate with judicial authority; burial place and plot granted at public expense.
EDCS-08200745.summary: This inscription records the grant of a burial plot at public expense to Tiberius Aulius Marinus, a magistrate.

EDCS-08200746.translation: Lucius Cassius Flavianus, son and grandson and great-grandson of Lucius, of the Maecia tribe, held the office of quaestor four times with aedilician power and as a magistrate with judicial authority. He lived 40 years. What was decided concerning the honorable funeral of Lucius Cassius Flavianus, as he wished when alive. They decreed as follows: When Lucius Cassius Flavianus, a distinguished Roman knight, had fulfilled the reputation of a truly outstanding and rare citizen through his industry, character, and eloquence, he was worthy of lasting remembrance, and [---] of the most severe grief [---].
EDCS-08200746.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Lucius Cassius Flavianus, a Roman knight, praising his character and achievements and indicating the decision of the local council regarding his funeral arrangements.

EDCS-08200747.translation: Eucratidas, son of Pisidamus, from Rhodes, an Epicurean philosopher; the place was granted at public expense by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08200747.summary: This inscription records the grant of a burial place at public expense to Eucratidas, a Rhodian philosopher.

EDCS-08200749.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Mercellia Festa, daughter of Titus. Publius Gerellanus Fuscus to his excellent mother. The town council decreed a statue, funeral, and burial plot for her at public expense. She lived 38 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08200749.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Publius Gerellanus Fuscus for his mother, Mercellia Festa, stating that the town council granted her a statue, funeral, and burial plot at public expense.

EDCS-08200750.translation: To Lucius Publicius; the fellow townsmen [---]
EDCS-08200750.summary: This is a dedication by fellow townsmen to Lucius Publicius, likely a funerary or honorific inscription.

EDCS-08200752.translation: Marcus Cocceius Magnus, Augustalis; he lived 60 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08200752.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Cocceius Magnus, an Augustalis, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-08200754.translation: Sextus Pompeius Magnus Mercurialis, freedman of a woman; he lived 65 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08200754.summary: This is a tombstone for Sextus Pompeius Magnus Mercurialis, a freedman, who lived 65 years.

EDCS-08200755.translation: Caius Pomponius Symphorus, Mercurialis; he lived 41 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08200755.summary: This is a tombstone for Caius Pomponius Symphorus, associated with the cult of Mercury, who lived 41 years.

EDCS-08200756.translation: To the Divine Shades. Publius Sextius Successus, Augustalis; he lived 80 years. He is buried here. Funeral, burial place, and plot granted at public expense by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08200756.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication for Publius Sextius Successus, an Augustalis, who lived 80 years, and records that his burial plot was granted at public expense.

EDCS-08200758.translation: To the Divine Shades. Maximus, public overseer of the Brindisians; he lived 50 years. To his well-deserving wife, Nemestronia Philaenis.
EDCS-08200758.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Maximus, a public overseer, for his wife Nemestronia Philaenis, noting his age.

EDCS-08200760.translation: Gaius Clodius, a soldier from Brundisium.
EDCS-08200760.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Clodius, a soldier from Brundisium.

EDCS-08200761.translation: Gaius Hostilius Hypatus, a businessman from Bithynia, lived for 34 years.
EDCS-08200761.summary: This tombstone records the death of Gaius Hostilius Hypatus, a businessman from Bithynia, who died at the age of 34.

EDCS-08200762.translation: Lucius Accaus Clar, lived for 30 years, is buried here.
EDCS-08200762.summary: This tombstone records that Lucius Accaus Clar was buried here after living for 30 years.

EDCS-08200763.translation: Quintus Acerratius Artemidorus lived for 40 years and is buried here. Acerratia Trophime lived for 60 years and is buried here.
EDCS-08200763.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Quintus Acerratius Artemidorus (died aged 40) and Acerratia Trophime (died aged 60).

EDCS-08200764.translation: Acerretina Salvia lived for 50 years and is buried here.
EDCS-08200764.summary: This tombstone records that Acerretina Salvia was buried here after living for 50 years.

EDCS-08200765.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Aemilius Philumenus lived for 42 years and is buried here. Volumnia Festa for herself and her deserving husband.
EDCS-08200765.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the spirits of the dead, recording the burial of Lucius Aemilius Philumenus (died aged 42) and a dedication by Volumnia Festa for herself and her husband.

EDCS-08200766.translation: Aemilia Epicarpia lived for 16 years and is buried here.
EDCS-08200766.summary: This tombstone records that Aemilia Epicarpia was buried here after living for 16 years.

EDCS-08200767.translation: Agathopus, belonging to Iulius Niger, aged 28, is buried here.
EDCS-08200767.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Agathopus, a freedman of Iulius Niger, who died at the age of 28.

EDCS-08200768.translation: Alcimus, slave of Aulus Laelius, lived for 18 years and is buried here.
EDCS-08200768.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Alcimus, a slave of Aulus Laelius, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-08200769.translation: Alcimus, vicarius of Tyrannus Mutro, lived for 12 years and is buried here.
EDCS-08200769.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Alcimus, the vicarius (substitute or representative) of Tyrannus Mutro, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-08200770.translation: Anatole lived for 2 years and 4 months; she lies here.
EDCS-08200770.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Anatole, a child who lived for 2 years and 4 months.

EDCS-08200771.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Anaupauma, who lived for 2 years.

EDCS-08200772.translation: Ania Fortunata, freedwoman of Lucius, lived for 35 years; she lies here.
EDCS-08200772.summary: A tombstone for Ania Fortunata, who lived for 35 years.

EDCS-08200773.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Antonius...
EDCS-08200773.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed, beginning with the name Marcus Antonius.

EDCS-08200777.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Aruntius Papia, freedman of Lucius, lies here.
EDCS-08200777.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Aruntius Papia, freedman of Lucius, indicating he lies here.

EDCS-08200778.translation: Marcus Aruntius Imio lived for 40 years; he lies here.
EDCS-08200778.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Aruntius Imio, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-08200779.translation: ...ius Auctus lived for 50 years; he lies here. ...for a deserving grandfather and father.
EDCS-08200779.summary: A funerary inscription for ...ius Auctus, who lived 50 years, and mentions deserving male ancestors, likely indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-08200780.translation: Avidia Hesperis lived for 35 years; she lies here.
EDCS-08200780.summary: A tombstone for Avidia Hesperis, who lived for 35 years.

EDCS-08200781.translation: Tiberius Aulius Sedatus, son of Tiberius.
EDCS-08200781.summary: An inscription recording the name and parentage of Tiberius Aulius Sedatus.

EDCS-08200782.translation: Titus Aurelius Pudens lived for 70 years; he lies here.
EDCS-08200782.summary: A tombstone for Titus Aurelius Pudens, who lived for 70 years.

EDCS-08200783.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Baebia Euche.

EDCS-08200784.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Biton Socratu, grandfather Hermeros Sabdi, lived 30 years.
EDCS-08200784.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Biton Socratu, grandson of Hermeros Sabdi, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-08200785.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Brundisina, recording her age and burial.

EDCS-08200786.text_edition: \n Caesellia Luci liberta Moschin sic vixit annos XLV hic sita Caesellia Luci filia Helspis sic vixit annos IV menses VI hic sita\n
EDCS-08200786.translation: Moschin, freedwoman of Lucius Caesellius, lived 45 years, buried here. Helspis, daughter of Lucius Caesellius, lived 4 years and 6 months, buried here.
EDCS-08200786.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for two women, Moschin and Helspis, recording their ages and burial places, with familial connections.

EDCS-08200787.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caesius Novianus lived 5 years and 7 months, buried here. Arruntia Procula [dedicated this] to her most devoted son.
EDCS-08200787.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Arruntia Procula to her son Caesius Novianus, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-08200788.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Caesia Callipolis.

EDCS-08200789.translation: Decimus Camianus Atime tus lived 55 years, buried here.
EDCS-08200789.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Decimus Camianus Atime tus.

EDCS-08200790.translation: Caninia Soteria lived 20 years, buried here.
EDCS-08200790.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Caninia Soteria.

EDCS-08200791.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cerellius Polycarpus lived 4 years, buried here.
EDCS-08200791.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan and burial of Cerellius Polycarpus.

EDCS-08200792.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Cervonia Aelian(a), his well-deserving wife, Cautinus Restitutus [dedicated this]. She lived 22 years, 10 months, and 13 days.
EDCS-08200792.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Cautinus Restitutus to his wife Cervonia Aelian(a), recording her lifespan.

EDCS-08200793.translation: Chrysogonus, freedman of Claudius Bassus, lived 35 years. Frunita, his wife, placed [this tomb].
EDCS-08200793.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Chrysogonus, who lived 35 years, set up by his wife Frunita.

EDCS-08200794.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Cincius Capito, son of Lucius, lived 40 years and 11 months. Claudia Urbana [placed this tomb] for her husband, an excellent man, and for herself.
EDCS-08200794.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Cincius Capito, who lived 40 years and 11 months, set up by his wife Claudia Urbana for them both.

EDCS-08200795.translation: To the departed spirits. Tiberius Claudius Eucaerus lived 3 years. Claudius Graptus lived 75 years. They are buried here.
EDCS-08200795.summary: A funerary epitaph for two individuals, Tiberius Claudius Eucaerus (3 years) and Claudius Graptus (75 years).

EDCS-08200796.translation: To the departed spirits. Claudius Nilus lived 41 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08200796.summary: A funerary epitaph for Claudius Nilus, who lived 41 years.

EDCS-08200797.translation: Claudia Ta[---] lived 80 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08200797.summary: A funerary epitaph for Claudia Ta[---], who lived 80 years.

EDCS-08200798.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph for Lucius Clodius.

EDCS-08200799.translation: Quintus Clodius Symphorus lived 5 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08200799.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Clodius Symphorus, who lived 5 years.

EDCS-08200800.translation: To the departed spirits. Clodia Soleris lived 9 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08200800.summary: A funerary epitaph for Clodia Soleris, who lived 9 years.

EDCS-08200801.translation: Syntyche, freedwoman of Corfidia, lived 130 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08200801.summary: A funerary epitaph for Syntyche, freedwoman of Corfidia, who lived an exceptionally long life of 130 years.

EDCS-08200803.translation: Lucius Cuspius Primus lived 25 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08200803.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Cuspius Primus, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-08200804.translation: Ennenia Victoria lived 50 years; here she lies.
EDCS-08200804.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ennenia Victoria, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-08200805.translation: Epictetus, slave of Gaius Cassius Iustus, lived 25 years; here he lies.
EDCS-08200805.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Epictetus, a slave, recording his lifespan and burial, mentioning his owner.

EDCS-08200806.translation: To the departed spirits. Epiteuxis lived 35 years; here he lies.
EDCS-08200806.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits for Epiteuxis, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-08200807.translation: To the departed spirits. Ergasiae Ulpiae Lacenae, slave, lived 50 years; here she lies.
EDCS-08200807.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits for Ergasiae Ulpiae Lacenae, a slave, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-08200808.translation: Eytychia lived 35 years; here she lies.
EDCS-08200808.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Eytychia, recording her lifespan and burial.

EDCS-08200809.translation: Eutychus, slave of Titus Marcellus Fuscus, lived 15 years; here he lies.
EDCS-08200809.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Eutychus, a slave, recording his lifespan and burial, mentioning his owner.

EDCS-08200810.translation: [Gaius] Fabius Dasius, freedman of Gaius, lived 50 years; here he lies.
EDCS-08200810.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Fabius Dasius, a freedman, recording his lifespan and burial, and identifying his former owner.

EDCS-08200811.translation: Lucius Laenius Soterichus lived 55 years; here he lies.
EDCS-08200811.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Laenius Soterichus, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-08200812.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Faonia Primigenia, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-08200813.translation: Gaius Falerius Asyncritus lived 60 years; here he lies.
EDCS-08200813.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Falerius Asyncritus, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-08200815.translation: Flavia Syntyche, aged fifteen, is buried here.
EDCS-08200815.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavia Syntyche, indicating her age and burial.

EDCS-08200816.translation: Quintus Fufius Deceberillus, son of Quintus, lived five years and is buried here; son of Laeniaes Pastades.
EDCS-08200816.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Fufius Deceberillus, recording his age, parentage, and burial.

EDCS-08200817.translation: Fufia Secunda, freedwoman of a woman, lived sixty years and is buried here.
EDCS-08200817.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fufia Secunda, noting her freed status, age, and burial.

EDCS-08200818.translation: Aulus Gabinius Hyginus, father; Gabinia Magaria, aged sixty; Aulus Gabinius Tarpeius, aged eleven.
EDCS-08200818.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gabinia Magaria and Aulus Gabinius Tarpeius, providing their ages.

EDCS-08200819.translation: Aulus Gabinius Secundus, aged sixty, is buried here.
EDCS-08200819.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Gabinius Secundus, indicating his age and burial.

EDCS-08200820.translation: Gavinua Auge lived thirty-two years and is buried here.
EDCS-08200820.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gavinua Auge, recording her age and burial.

EDCS-08200821.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Hedone, freedwoman of Publius, lived thirty years and is buried here. To Isidorus, her most excellent mother.
EDCS-08200821.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Hedone by her mother Isidorus, recording her age and burial.

EDCS-08200822.translation: Publius Gifinius Sotericus is buried here.
EDCS-08200822.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Gifinius Sotericus, indicating his burial.

EDCS-08200823.translation: Graecalia Prima lived twenty-seven years and is buried here.
EDCS-08200823.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Graecalia Prima, recording her age and burial.

EDCS-08200824.translation: Lucius Granius Corinthus lived thirty-five years and is buried here.
EDCS-08200824.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Granius Corinthus, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-08200826.translation: Hesper, wife of Marcus Aquillus, lived 40 years, lies here. Tiberius Claudius Fructus lived 40 years, lies here.
EDCS-08200826.summary: This is a double funerary inscription recording the ages at death of two individuals, likely a couple, from an unknown date.

EDCS-08200827.translation: Julius Fufius, son of Gaius, lived 14 years, lie here.
EDCS-08200827.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the age at death of Julius Fufius, from an unknown date.

EDCS-08200828.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Julius Hermes lived 56 years. Kaninia Tryphaena lived 60 years, lies here.
EDCS-08200828.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Julius Hermes and Kaninia Tryphaena, recording their ages at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200829.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Julius Laeander lived 9 years, lies here.
EDCS-08200829.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age at death of Lucius Julius Laeander, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200830.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulia Cosmia lived 20 years. Iulius Pisto placed [this] for his relative.
EDCS-08200830.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Iulius Pisto dedicated a monument for Iulia Cosmia, recording her age at death, from an unknown date.

EDCS-08200831.translation: Iulia Phoebe, freedwoman of Gaius, lived 60 years.
EDCS-08200831.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the age at death of Iulia Phoebe, a freedwoman, from an unknown date.

EDCS-08200832.translation: Iulia Prima, freedwoman of Iulus, lived 65 years, lies here.
EDCS-08200832.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age at death of Iulia Prima, a freedwoman, from an unknown date.

EDCS-08200834.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iuventia Primigenia lived 60 years, lies here. Ursilla [placed this for the] deserving one.
EDCS-08200834.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Ursilla dedicated a monument for Iuventia Primigenia, recording her age at death, from an unknown date.

EDCS-08200835.translation: Marcus Lollius Arphocras while alive made this for himself and his wife Paelinae and for his freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants. This is a monument or tomb; it will not be followed by an heir, nor will it be available for rental for one hundred years. If anyone acts against this, their property will belong to the public property of the people of Brundisium.
EDCS-08200835.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription set up by Marcus Lollius Arphocras for himself and his family, including stringent conditions regarding future use and penalties for violation, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200836.translation: Marcus Lollius Arphocras while alive made [this] for Lollia Paelinae
EDCS-08200836.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary monument inscription started by Marcus Lollius Arphocras for Lollia Paelinae, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200837.translation: Gaius Lucceius Primigenius lived 14 years, lies here.
EDCS-08200837.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial place of Gaius Lucceius Primigenius.

EDCS-08200838.translation: [Son of] Scalus, of the Mae(cia) tribe, lies here, and Grania, daughter of Sextus, his wife, lies here.
EDCS-08200838.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Scalus and his wife Grania.

EDCS-08200839.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial place of Mamia Zozima.

EDCS-08200840.translation: Tyche, freedwoman of the woman Maria, lived 35 years, lived L [years?]. To Lucius Domitius Epagathus, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-08200840.summary: A funerary inscription for Tyche, a freedwoman, with an unclear reference to Lucius Domitius Epagathus.

EDCS-08200841.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Martialis, a slave.

EDCS-08200842.translation: Lucius Messius Herma lived 70 years, lies here.
EDCS-08200842.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial place of Lucius Messius Herma.

EDCS-08200843.translation: Lucius Mindius Felicio lived 3 years and 3 months, lies here.
EDCS-08200843.summary: A funerary inscription recording the short lifespan and burial place of Lucius Mindius Felicio.

EDCS-08200844.translation: Lucius Mindius Primogenes lived 10 years and 5 days, lies here.
EDCS-08200844.summary: A funerary inscription recording the short lifespan and burial place of Lucius Mindius Primogenes.

EDCS-08200845.translation: Marcus Mussienus Restitutus lived 28 years; miserable mother. Marcus Mussienus Phoebus lived 60 years.
EDCS-08200845.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespans of Marcus Mussienus Restitutus and Marcus Mussienus Phoebus, possibly his mother and son.

EDCS-08200846.translation: Myrine lived 17 years. Eutychia, who is also called Buttin, lived 3 years. They lie here. Eros dedicated [this] to his dearest wife and daughter.
EDCS-08200846.summary: A funerary monument set up by Eros for his wife Myrine (also known as Buttin) and daughter Eutychia.

EDCS-08200847.summary: A tombstone for Negila Exspectata who lived 30 years.

EDCS-08200848.translation: Negilia Nigella, freedwoman of Lucius, and Valeria Zosara, freedwoman of Lucius, [set this up for] 15 feet in front, 20 feet in land.
EDCS-08200848.summary: An epitaph for Negilia Nigella and Valeria Zosara, specifying the dimensions of a burial plot.

EDCS-08200849.translation: Vitalia, freedwoman of a woman (Negilia), lived 60 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-08200849.summary: A tombstone for Vitalia, a freedwoman, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-08200850.translation: Son of Marcus, Nepos Clemens, [set this up for] Quintus and his brother [---] and for his father Symo and his mother Sympherusa.
EDCS-08200850.summary: A funerary dedication possibly for family members, with missing names and titles.

EDCS-08200851.translation: To the Divine Manes. Novia Repentina lived 45 years. Valerius Adauctus [set this up] for his most devoted wife; she is buried here.
EDCS-08200851.summary: A tombstone for Novia Repentina, erected by her husband Valerius Adauctus.

EDCS-08200852.text_edition: \n Caius Octavius Felix vixit annos L hic situs Vivia Vistulla hic sita cum suo coniuge\n
EDCS-08200852.translation: Gaius Octavius Felix lived 50 years, he is buried here. Vivia Vistulla is buried here with her husband.
EDCS-08200852.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Octavius Felix and Vivia Vistulla, a married couple.

EDCS-08200853.translation: Catecusa set up this memorial for Otavius Graphicus, her well-deserving comrade, who lived 33 years.
EDCS-08200853.summary: A memorial erected by Catecusa for her comrade Otavius Graphicus who lived 33 years.

EDCS-08200854.translation: To the Divine Manes. Octavius Pina lived [3]15 years; his wife set this up for the deserving one.
EDCS-08200854.summary: A tombstone for Octavius Pina, erected by his wife.

EDCS-08200855.translation: Octavia Sytyche, freedwoman of Octavia, lived 40 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-08200855.summary: A tombstone for Octavia Sytyche, a freedwoman, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-08200856.translation: Octavia Tyche, freedwoman of Caius, is buried here.
EDCS-08200856.summary: An epitaph for Octavia Tyche, a freedwoman.

EDCS-08200857.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of a young child named Gaius Oppius Agatho.

EDCS-08200858.translation: Publius Pacilius Agathopus lived 55 years; here he lies, [for] Pacilia, daughter of Gnaeus.
EDCS-08200858.summary: A tombstone for Publius Pacilius Agathopus, likely related to Pacilia, daughter of Gnaeus.

EDCS-08200859.translation: Publius Pacilius Silvanus, freedman of Publius, lived 60 years; here he lies.
EDCS-08200859.summary: A tombstone for Publius Pacilius Silvanus, a freedman, indicating his age and burial.
