EDCS-64500112.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Considius Primigenius, including a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-64500113.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Pithane lived 20 years. Eutychus for his well-deserving female slave.
EDCS-64500113.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Pithane, who lived 20 years, by her owner Eutychus.

EDCS-64500114.translation: Faonia Ursilla lived 60 years. Helpistus for a deserving person.
EDCS-64500114.summary: A funerary epitaph for Faonia Ursilla, who lived 60 years, with a dedication by Helpistus.

EDCS-64500115.translation: Ampliata, Postumus' slave, lived 28 years. Here she lies. Fortunatus for a deserving person.
EDCS-64500115.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ampliata, a slave of Postumus, who lived 28 years, with a dedication by Fortunatus.

EDCS-64500116.translation: Manius Canuleius Exspectatus lived 70 years. Here he lies.
EDCS-64500116.summary: A funerary epitaph for Manius Canuleius Exspectatus, who lived 70 years.

EDCS-64500118.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Hermeros.

EDCS-64500119.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Oriens, identified as a father.

EDCS-64500120.translation: Agatho. Euni. Here he lies. Lived 50 years.
EDCS-64500120.summary: A funerary epitaph for Agatho, also mentioning Euni, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-64500427.translation: Numerius Afinius Flaccus, son of Numerius, grandson of Lucius, from the Alban district, saw to it that an altar be made at his own expense and approved it.
EDCS-64500427.summary: An inscription recording the construction and approval of a public altar by Numerius Afinius Flaccus in the Alban district.

EDCS-64500428.translation: Gnaeus Suellius Classetianus Bassus lived 40 years and 6 months.
EDCS-64500428.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Gnaeus Suellius Classetianus Bassus.

EDCS-64500494.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Helvia Piralis.

EDCS-64500495.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Caledia Syminia... lived 5 years. Here she lies.
EDCS-64500495.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caledia Syminia, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-64500496.translation: ... daughter of Quint[a] ... lived 10 years ... daughter of Caius, Herma[...], to her husband ...
EDCS-64500496.summary: A fragmented funerary epitaph mentioning a daughter, her lifespan, and her husband.

EDCS-64500559.translation: ... of Marcus Ae[--] ... slave ... lived 75 years.
EDCS-64500559.summary: A fragmented funerary epitaph for a slave who lived 75 years.

EDCS-64600210.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Vibiaes Cleopatra lived 50 years. Here she lies. Numerius Vibius Herma lived 60 years. Here he lies.
EDCS-64600210.summary: A double funerary epitaph for Vibiaes Cleopatra and Numerius Vibius Herma.

EDCS-64600211.translation: Publius Tettius Vitalis lived 35 years. Here he lies.
EDCS-64600211.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Tettius Vitalis.

EDCS-64600212.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Anthestia Marcia lived 19 years. Anthestia Semne made this for herself and her.
EDCS-64600212.summary: A funerary epitaph where Anthestia Semne sets up a monument for Anthestia Marcia and herself.

EDCS-64600213.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Valeria Tertia lived 40 years and 8 months. Bestales made this for his well-deserving wife. Here she lies.
EDCS-64600213.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Tertia, set up by her husband Bestales.

EDCS-64800022.translation: Gamice lived 4 years and 5 months. Here she lies.
EDCS-64800022.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child named Gamice.

EDCS-64800025.translation: Aemilius Classicus lived 40 years, is buried here.
EDCS-64800025.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilius Classicus, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-64800026.translation: Titus Latinius Epaphora, freedman of Titus, lived 60 years, is buried here.
EDCS-64800026.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Latinius Epaphora, a freedman, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-64800027.translation: Audia Ethe lived 20 years, is buried here.
EDCS-64800027.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Audia Ethe, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-64800028.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Audia Ianuaris, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-64800029.translation: Cerealis lived 30 years, is buried here.
EDCS-64800029.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cerealis, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-64800030.translation: Amaranthus, a public slave and gardener, lived 50 years, is buried here.
EDCS-64800030.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Amaranthus, a public slave and gardener, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-64800031.translation: Publius Lucedius Felix Maior, freedman of Publius, an Augustalis and Mercurialis, lived 80 years, is buried here.
EDCS-64800031.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Lucedius Felix Maior, a freedman and member of the Augustalis and Mercurialis collegia, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-64800032.translation: Cinuria is buried here; she lived 8 years.
EDCS-64800032.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cinuria, who died at the age of 8.

EDCS-64800033.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Titus Linius Asiaticus, who died at the age of 51.

EDCS-64800034.translation: Gaius Herennius Secundus, freedman of Gaius, lived 26 years, is buried here.
EDCS-64800034.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Herennius Secundus, a freedman, who died at the age of 26.

EDCS-64800035.translation: Cerdo, freedman of Doraes, lived 60 years. Here he lies.
EDCS-64800035.summary: A tombstone for Cerdo, a freedman, stating his age and burial place.

EDCS-64800409.translation: To the departed spirits. Felicitas lived 21 years. Felicissimus made this for his beloved nurse, who lies here.
EDCS-64800409.summary: A tombstone dedicated by Felicissimus to his nurse Felicitas, stating her age at death.

EDCS-64900343.translation: Publius Servilius Stabilio lived 90 years. Farewell.
EDCS-64900343.summary: A tombstone for Publius Servilius Stabilio, stating his age at death.

EDCS-64900344.translation: Decimus Daburrus, freedman of Decimus Primio.
EDCS-64900344.summary: An inscription identifying Decimus Daburrus, freedman of Decimus Primio.

EDCS-64900345.translation: Aria, freedwoman of Publius Rasius, lived 70 years.
EDCS-64900345.summary: A tombstone for Aria, freedwoman of Publius Rasius, stating her age at death.

EDCS-64900384.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Epidius Verus, an Augustalis.
EDCS-64900384.summary: A tombstone fragment for Gaius Epidius Verus, identified as an Augustalis.

EDCS-64900385.translation: Of Titus Octavius Antiochus, Titus Octavius Gratus, Octavia Graeca, Octavia Laeta, 12 feet in the field and 12 feet in the front.
EDCS-64900385.summary: A boundary inscription related to a property owned by Titus Octavius Antiochus and others, specifying dimensions.

EDCS-64900386.translation: For Lucius Lucilius Lupus, son of Lucius, of the Stellatina tribe, aedile, prefect of the engineers. Lucilia Faustilla, his mother, for herself and for Gnaeus Rustius Nedymus.
EDCS-64900386.summary: A dedication by Lucilia Faustilla for her son Lucius Lucilius Lupus and for Gnaeus Rustius Nedymus, listing Lupus's offices.

EDCS-64900388.translation: Herennia Hilara, freedwoman of Caius, for herself and her family, 12 feet in the front, 16 feet in the field.
EDCS-64900388.summary: A funerary inscription for Herennia Hilara, specifying plot dimensions for her and her family.

EDCS-64900390.translation: Secunda, his wife, made this for her deserving husband with whom she lived 15 years.
EDCS-64900390.summary: A tombstone dedicated by Secunda to her husband, stating the duration of their marriage.

EDCS-64900659.summary: An epitaph recording the lifespan of Tursus.

EDCS-64900890.translation: To the departed spirits. Antyllus lived 6 years and is buried here. Despoina lived 4 years. Graptus made this for his children.
EDCS-64900890.summary: A funerary inscription for Antyllus and Despoina, with Graptus as the dedicator.

EDCS-64900891.translation: Severus Aug(ustalis), freedman of Publius Bussienus Pothus, lived 45 years and is buried here.
EDCS-64900891.summary: An epitaph for Severus Aug(ustalis), noting his freedman status and lifespan.

EDCS-64900892.translation: Fufia Luchnis, fulfilling her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-64900892.summary: An inscription recording Fufia Luchnis fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-64901009.translation: To the departed spirits. Suellius Primus made this for his wife Crispia Amand(a), with whom he lived well for 70 years, deservedly.
EDCS-64901009.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Suellius Primus to his wife Crispia Amand(a), noting their long and good marriage.

EDCS-64901010.translation: To the departed spirits. Athanasius and Veredia made this for their deserving freedman Gaius Julius Sabinianus.
EDCS-64901010.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by Athanasius and Veredia to their freedman Gaius Julius Sabinianus.

EDCS-65100089.translation: Marcus Clodius Primogenes lived 10 years. Clodius Anycet[us...]
EDCS-65100089.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Clodius Primogenes, with a partial record for Clodius Anycet[us].

EDCS-65200030.translation: Onirus, slave of Isaurica, lived 55 years.
EDCS-65200030.summary: An epitaph recording the lifespan and status of Onirus.

EDCS-65300023.translation: To the departed spirits. Aulus Pettius [---]ntinus, [---] C(a)eparius(?) V[---]
EDCS-65300023.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription with names that are largely lost.

EDCS-65300024.translation: Artemidorus and Ferox made this for their deserving friend Glypte, slave of Pothinus, who lived 50 years.
EDCS-65300024.summary: A funerary inscription for Glypte, a slave, dedicated by her friends Artemidorus and Ferox.

EDCS-65300026.translation: Helpis Hordionia lived 80 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-65300026.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Helpis Hordionia, who lived 80 years.

EDCS-65300028.translation: Amasonicus, the slave of Asinius, lived 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-65300028.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Amasonicus, a slave, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-65300029.translation: Nemetoria Phoebe lived 45 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-65300029.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Nemetoria Phoebe, who lived 45 years.

EDCS-65300030.translation: Titinia Saturnina lived 50 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-65300030.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titinia Saturnina, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-65300031.translation: Marcus Plotius Sygnomus lived 37 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-65300031.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Plotius Sygnomus, who lived 37 years.

EDCS-65300033.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucretia Eutychis made this for her son Decimus Lucretius Super, who lived 26 years, a well-deserving son.
EDCS-65300033.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Lucretia Eutychis dedicated a tomb for her son, Decimus Lucretius Super, who died at age 26.

EDCS-65300039.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aquilia Iulia, who lived 75 years, 3 months, and 5 days, is buried here. Her daughter made this for her well-deserving mother, and her granddaughter placed it for her well-deserving grandmother.
EDCS-65300039.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Aquilia Iulia, who lived 75 years, 3 months, and 5 days, and was commemorated by her daughter and granddaughter.

EDCS-65300041.translation: Gaius Screvius Hilarus is buried here. Claudia Prima is buried here.
EDCS-65300041.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Gaius Screvius Hilarus and Claudia Prima.

EDCS-65300043.translation: Lyde lived 1 year; she is buried here.
EDCS-65300043.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lyde, who lived 1 year.

EDCS-65300077.translation: Lucius Pupius, son of Quintus, twice a magistrate with judicial power, paved [this area] with his own money for the games.
EDCS-65300077.summary: This inscription records that Lucius Pupius, son of Quintus, a local magistrate, funded the paving of an area for public games.

EDCS-65300078.translation: To Marcus Casineius Vassius Passenillianus Titus, son of Marcus, of the Stellatina tribe, a man of the highest distinction.
EDCS-65300078.summary: This is a funerary dedication honoring Marcus Casineius Vassius Passenillianus Titus, likely by his family or a patron.

EDCS-65300084.translation: To Gaius Canellius Rufus, their son, who lived 35 years. Gaius Canellius Secundus, his father, and Egnatia Rufina, his mother, made this for him as deserving well.
EDCS-65300084.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by parents for their deceased son, Gaius Canellius Rufus, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-65300086.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sextus Hellenius Liberalis and Attia Laodice, parents, for their sweetest daughter Helleni, daughter of Sextus, of the Sextina tribe.
EDCS-65300086.summary: This is a funerary dedication from parents to their beloved daughter, Helleni, honoring her memory and likely placed on her tomb.

EDCS-65300087.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, sacred. Gaius Sempronius Fidelis and Sempronia Primigenia made this for their most excellent father, Gaius Sempronius Restitutus, son of Gaius, of the Stellatina tribe.
EDCS-65300087.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication from children to their highly esteemed father, Gaius Sempronius Restitutus.

EDCS-65300088.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Cocceia Modesta for her husband Lucius Trebelenus Hospes, with whom she lived 33 years, to him deserving well.
EDCS-65300088.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by a wife to her husband, Lucius Trebelenus Hospes, noting their long marriage and his worth.

EDCS-65300171.translation: Lucius [---], son of Gilius [---], lived 35 years; he lies here.
EDCS-65300171.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of a deceased individual named Lucius, who lived 35 years and is buried here.

EDCS-65300172.translation: Decimus [---], lived [---] years; he lies here [---] deserving [---].
EDCS-65300172.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph that mentions an individual named Decimus and indicates he is buried here.

EDCS-65300173.translation: Pathria Ampliata lived 40 years; she lies here, a deserving comrade.
EDCS-65300173.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Pathria Ampliata at age 40 and identifies her as a deserving 'contubernalis' (often indicating a fellow soldier or spouse).

EDCS-65300174.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Phaleres, who died at the age of 16, stating that he is buried here.

EDCS-65300175.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and young age (7 years) of a deceased child, Paezusa.

EDCS-65300176.translation: Lucius Venerius lived 35 years and is buried here.
EDCS-65300176.summary: A tombstone recording the age and burial of Lucius Venerius.

EDCS-65300178.summary: A tombstone fragment mentioning Marcus Ianuarius and his age at death.

EDCS-65300179.translation: [---]us Firmus and his mother lived 55 years and are buried here.
EDCS-65300179.summary: A tombstone recording the ages of Firmus and his mother.

EDCS-65300180.translation: Caius Vetius Secundus lived 60 years and [is buried] here.
EDCS-65300180.summary: A tombstone fragment noting the age of Caius Vetius Secundus.

EDCS-65300181.translation: Lucius Tampanius Optatus lived 6 years and 3 months and is buried [here] [---].
EDCS-65300181.summary: A tombstone recording the age and burial of Lucius Tampanius Optatus.

EDCS-65300183.translation: To the departed spirits. Sabinianus lived 13 years and is buried here. Aprilis made this for his well-deserving freedman.
EDCS-65300183.summary: A funerary dedication from Aprilis to his freedman Sabinianus, specifying the latter's age.

EDCS-65300184.translation: Venerius lived 2 years and is buried here.
EDCS-65300184.summary: A tombstone recording the age and burial of Venerius.

EDCS-65300185.translation: Gnaeus Pompeius Secundus Potamo lived 80 years [---].
EDCS-65300185.summary: A tombstone fragment mentioning Gnaeus Pompeius Secundus Potamo and his age.

EDCS-65300186.translation: Lucius Clodius Primigenius lived 50 years and is buried here. To the departed spirits. Hordionia Helpis made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-65300186.summary: A funerary dedication from Hordionia Helpis to her husband Lucius Clodius Primigenius, stating his age and burial.

EDCS-65300187.translation: [---] Chione is buried here. [---]scarpa(?) and Eutychia [---].
EDCS-65300187.summary: A tombstone fragment mentioning individuals named Chione, Mascarpus (?), and Eutychia.

EDCS-65300188.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Aquius Satyr lived 60 years, he lies here. Iulia Fortunata made (this) for her deserving husband and for herself; she lived 50 years, she lies here. And their children made (this) for their deserving parents.
EDCS-65300188.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Aquius Satyr and Iulia Fortunata, likely dating to the 2nd century CE, set up by their children.

EDCS-65300189.translation: Grapte lived 5 years, she lies here.
EDCS-65300189.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for a young girl named Grapte, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65300190.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Artoria Aphrodisia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65300191.translation: Marcus Apro/nius Mallus, son of Marcus, lived 60 years.
EDCS-65300191.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Marcus Apronius Mallus, a freedman, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65300192.translation: Acerronia Eleutheria lived 75 years, she lies here.
EDCS-65300192.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Acerronia Eleutheria, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65300193.translation: Marcus Publilius Lucrio lived 105 years, dear to his family, he lies here.
EDCS-65300193.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Publilius Lucrio, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-65300218.translation: To Valeria Maximilla, dearest and incomparable wife, who lived 22 years and 11 days, a deserving woman. Noricus, a tax collector from the colonists of Beneventum, made (this) for her and for himself while alive.
EDCS-65300218.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Maximilla, set up by her husband Noricus, a tax collector, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-65800081.translation: [Mar]cia Eutychia, freedwoman of Lucius, lived 25 years, she lies here.
EDCS-65800081.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Marcia Eutychia, a freedwoman, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-66800021.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely the beginning of a name and filiation, possibly from a funerary monument, dating to the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-67400495.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 7th time, consul for the 3rd time, restored the Appian Way, which had been lost due to age, for 15,270 paces, by contributing 457,000 sesterces, to which the landowners contributed 569,100 sesterces, he made (it).
EDCS-67400495.summary: This is a monumental inscription from the reign of Emperor Hadrian (117-138 CE) recording the restoration of the Appian Way.

EDCS-67800015.translation: Quintus Ant[---] and Publius [---], sons of Marcus, quinquennial officials, paved (this) with their own money.
EDCS-67800015.summary: This inscription records a paving project funded by two quinquennial officials, likely from the late Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-69000011.translation: To the divine shades. Gaius Aurunculeius Augustalis and Aurunculeia Cyane, most unhappy parents, made (this) for Gaius Aurunculeius Museus, son of Gaius, decurion of Caudium, quaestor.
EDCS-69000011.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by parents to their deceased son, who held civic offices in the town of Caudium, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-69000058.translation: Iunia Arbuscula, freedwoman of Lucius, willingly and deservedly paid her vow to Venus.
EDCS-69000058.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by a freedwoman to the goddess Venus, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-69000059.translation: [---] for 20 days [---]. Fortunatus made (this) for his most sweet [---] daughter. Sabinius Speratus [---] Zenodora made (this) for his [---] daughter, well deserving.
EDCS-69000059.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a funerary monument for daughters, with at least two individuals (Fortunatus and Sabinius Speratus) involved in dedicating it, dating possibly to the Imperial period.

EDCS-69000060.translation: Atilia Tertia, daughter of Gaius, [in width] 12 feet, [in depth] 12 feet.
EDCS-69000060.summary: This inscription likely marks the dimensions of a plot of land, possibly for burial or construction, dedicated by Atilia Tertia, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-69000061.translation: To Minatia Chia, freedwoman of Gaius, my mother.
EDCS-69000061.summary: This is a dedication to a mother named Minatia Chia, who was a freedwoman, by an unnamed individual, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-69000062.translation: To the divine shades. Quintus Octavius Genialis and Quintus Octavius Proculus made (this) for their mother Lucerina Proculla, well deserving.
EDCS-69000062.summary: This funerary monument was erected by two sons for their mother, Lucerina Proculla, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-69200210.translation: Quintus Baebius Festus, the Augustalis, his son Quintus Baebius Festus erected (this).
EDCS-69200210.summary: This monument was erected by a son for his father, Quintus Baebius Festus, who held the prestigious position of Augustalis, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-69200211.translation: For Marcus Licinius Pudentus and his wife Egregia.
EDCS-69200211.summary: This is a dedication for a couple, Marcus Licinius Pudentus and Egregia, likely a funerary or commemorative monument from the Imperial period.

EDCS-69200213.translation: To Quintus Gagilius Modestus, son of Quintus, of the Palatina tribe, aedile, prefect of the Fabri, quaestor, pontiff, duumvir, tribune of the Fourth Scythian Legion. Here, having lost his son on his birthday, he gave 1 denarius annually to the people of Beneventum and promised 400,000 sesterces, so that in perpetuity the memory of his son would be celebrated on the 12th day before the Kalends of May; similarly he gave 1 denarius annually to the villagers of the Aequi Tutici quarter and promised in perpetuity. The villagers of the Aequi Tutici quarter, from collected funds, gave a statue to their patron.
EDCS-69200213.summary: This extensive inscription records the benefactions of Quintus Gagilius Modestus, a prominent local official and military man in Beneventum, who established perpetual endowments for public celebrations in memory of his son and for the benefit of local villagers, with the villagers erecting a statue in his honor, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-69200215.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Secundus, Gaius Bennius Secundus, Gaius Julius Modestus, his sons, and Bennia Modesta made this for him, a well-deserving man, at their own expense.
EDCS-69200215.summary: A funerary monument erected by sons and a daughter for Gaius Julius Secundus.

EDCS-70800227.translation: lived 11 years. He is buried here. Aprilis and Edone made this for their sweetest son.
EDCS-70800227.summary: A funerary epitaph for a boy named [son], whose parents Aprilis and Edone erected it.

EDCS-70900188.translation: Zethus, steward of Basila, lived 62 years.
EDCS-70900188.summary: A funerary epitaph for Zethus, the steward of Basila, who died at age 62.

EDCS-70900421.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Prima lived 15 years. She is buried here. To the spirits of the departed. Nicostratus lived 6 years. He is buried here. The father placed this tombstone for his sons.
EDCS-70900421.summary: A double funerary epitaph for Prima and Nicostratus, with their father dedicating the monument to his sons.

EDCS-70900422.translation: Caesennia Calliope lived 2 years, 11 months, 20 days. She is buried here. For Caesennia Felicula, who lived 2 years, 11 months, 20 days. She is buried here.
EDCS-70900422.summary: A double funerary epitaph for two girls, Caesennia Calliope and Caesennia Felicula, who died at the same age.

EDCS-70900424.translation: Gaius Calvius Phoebus lived 28 years. He is buried here. His comrade made this at his own expense.
EDCS-70900424.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Calvius Phoebus, erected by his comrade.

EDCS-70900425.translation: Titus Flavius Fortunatus lived 20 years, 5 months. He is buried here. His most unfortunate mother Calvia Felicula made this for her most excellent son.
EDCS-70900425.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Fortunatus, erected by his mother.

EDCS-70900759.translation: Marcus Marsidius Eureticus lived 40 years. He is buried here. Baebia Phronime lived 30 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-70900759.summary: A double funerary epitaph for Marcus Marsidius Eureticus and Baebia Phronime, likely a couple or family members.

EDCS-70900807.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Tertulla lived 70 years. She is buried here. Clymaene made this for her well-deserving mother.
EDCS-70900807.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tertulla, erected by her daughter Clymaene.

EDCS-70900808.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ursilla lived 60 years. She is buried here. Her sons made this for her, a well-deserving woman.
EDCS-70900808.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ursilla, erected by her sons.

EDCS-70900989.translation: Gaius? Claudius, son of Gaius, aedile. Gaius Helvius Valens, son of Gaius, aedile.
EDCS-70900989.summary: This inscription records two men, possibly colleagues, identified by their names, patronymics, and the office of aedile.

EDCS-70901080.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Gorgis lived 11 years; she is buried here. Gaius Iulius Antoninus erected this for his well-deserving freedwoman.
EDCS-70901080.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Gorgis, who died at age 11, set up by her former master.

EDCS-70901081.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records the names of two individuals, possibly Antestius Mugio and another person.

EDCS-71000629.translation: To the departed spirits. Venerius, slave of the Republic of the people of Veretinum, lived 45 years [---] V[---]
EDCS-71000629.summary: This funerary inscription records the name, status as a public slave, and lifespan of Venerius, a slave of the Veretinum Republic.

EDCS-71000701.translation: To Silvanus of Valeriana. Felicianus, slave and agent of Gaius Betitius Pius, from a vow.
EDCS-71000701.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus by Felicianus, the slave and agent of Gaius Betitius Pius.

EDCS-71000739.translation: [---] he/she is buried here. Tertia his wife [---] they made.
EDCS-71000739.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription indicating that someone is buried here, with Tertia being the wife and others contributing to the monument.

EDCS-71000751.translation: Publius Licinius Restitutus, freedman of Publius, lived 30 years. Here lies Pontia Olilia, most unhappy mother, for her most devoted son.
EDCS-71000751.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Licinius Restitutus, who died at age 30, set up by his mother and stepfather.

EDCS-71200696.translation: Tagullius Faustus Quasillus for himself and his family and for Messia Psycharion.
EDCS-71200696.summary: This is an ownership or dedication inscription by Tagullius Faustus Quasillus for himself, his family, and Messia Psycharion.

EDCS-71200697.translation: Plot of Statius Pomponius, freedman of Statius Philotimus. In front 12 feet, in the field 14 feet. Have.
EDCS-71200697.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot of land belonging to Philotimus, freedman of Statius Pomponius.

EDCS-71200699.translation: For Minatia Firmilla, who lived 17 years. Her mother Munatia Lychoris, and her father Gaius Minatius Firmus, and her stepfather Gaius Minatius Primigenius erected this.
EDCS-71200699.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Minatia Firmilla at age 17 and was erected by her parents and stepfather.

EDCS-71200700.translation: Myrthale and Martialis placed [this for] their mother.
EDCS-71200700.summary: A funerary dedication by Myrthale and Martialis for their mother.

EDCS-72200193.translation: Marcus Cornelius Rufus, son of Publius, and Marcus Vineius, son of Caius, as a pair of IIviri iure dicundo, had the censorship building repaired at public expense by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-72200193.summary: A record of the repair of a censorship building by two municipal magistrates, Marcus Cornelius Rufus and Marcus Vineius.

EDCS-72300045.summary: A fragmentary inscription that appears to record a personal name, possibly Drusus, son of Tiberius.

EDCS-72300174.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Statius Liber, son, made [this] for Junia Daphnis, his most devoted and dearest mother.
EDCS-72300174.summary: A funerary monument erected by Lucius Statius Liber for his mother, Junia Daphnis.

EDCS-72300175.translation: [He/She] made [this] for his/her wife, Primenia, who deserved well.
EDCS-72300175.summary: A funerary dedication for a wife named Primenia.

EDCS-72300179.translation: [Name], daughter of Marcus, buried here, [for] Boetus and for himself, [her] comrade.
EDCS-72300179.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription indicating a burial for Boetus and for the dedicator, who was Boetus's comrade.

EDCS-72300190.translation: To the departed spirits. Callitanus lived 75 years. Rodia placed [this?]
EDCS-72300190.summary: A funerary epitaph for Callitanus, recording his age and a possible dedicator named Rodia.

EDCS-72300253.translation: [---], son of [---]ia Peta[---]. Publilius [---] [---] his heirs.
EDCS-72300253.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely recording heirs, possibly of a family named Publilius.

EDCS-72300254.translation: To the departed spirits. Festibus Servus lived 4 years.
EDCS-72300254.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child named Festibus Servus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-72300255.translation: To the departed spirits. Paphie, slave of Marcus, lived 80 years. Vitalis [placed this for his] mother who deserved well.
EDCS-72300255.summary: A funerary monument for Paphie, a slave, and her mother, who deserved well, erected by Vitalis.

EDCS-72500083.translation: To Marcus Trebatius Rufus, son of Gaius, of the Cornelia tribe; and to Marcus Trebatius Rufus, his son; and to Trebatia Procilla, mother, daughter of Publius, of the Cornelia tribe; and to their freedmen.
EDCS-72500083.summary: This epitaph commemorates multiple members of the Trebatius family, including parents and a son, and their freedmen.

EDCS-72500084.translation: Marcus Trebatius Philetus made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-72500084.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Marcus Trebatius Philetus for his wife.

EDCS-72500085.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudia Crescentina made this memorial for her husband Salvidius Aper, who deserved well.
EDCS-72500085.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Claudia Crescentina to her deserving husband, Salvidius Aper.

EDCS-72500086.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording a name and possibly a dedication.

EDCS-72500088.translation: Gnaeus Ofillius Euhemer, freedman of Gnaeus, made this while alive for himself and for Ofillia Chresta.
EDCS-72500088.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gnaeus Ofillius Euhemer and Ofillia Chresta, made by Euhemer himself.

EDCS-72500090.translation: Marcus Munatius Eros, freedman of Marcus, for himself and for Marcus Munatius Plancinus, son of Marcus, and Pollia (?).
EDCS-72500090.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Munatius Eros and other family members, made by Eros himself.

EDCS-72500091.translation: To the spirits (?) of the departed. Messia Secundina made this for her most sweet daughter, Messia Fortunata.
EDCS-72500091.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a mother, Messia Secundina, to her beloved daughter, Messia Fortunata.

EDCS-72500092.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Luperci made this for their father, who deserved well.
EDCS-72500092.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Luperci to their deserving father.

EDCS-72500093.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Eggius [---] made this memorial for his wife (?).
EDCS-72500093.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary dedication, likely by Eggius to his wife.

EDCS-72500094.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [---] Eutychia, who lived 58 years. Marcellus [---] made this for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-72500094.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Eutychia at 58 years old and is a dedication by Marcellus.

EDCS-72500095.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Claudius Felicinus, his most unfortunate son, Claudius Felicio made this.
EDCS-72500095.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudius Felicinus, set up by his father of the same name, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500096.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Cinnamis, aged 15, who was nursed by [---]; to Amaryllis, [---] Vibius [set this up].
EDCS-72500096.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cinnamis, aged 15, and Amaryllis, set up by Vibius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500097.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Caesellius Firminus, his most dutiful father.
EDCS-72500097.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caesellius Firminus, set up by his son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500098.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Mother, father, and daughter made this for Benedicta, deserving of good.
EDCS-72500098.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Benedicta, set up by her parents and daughter, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500099.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Baebius Successus and his wife Secundina Primus, his children, made this for Baebius Successus, deserving of good.
EDCS-72500099.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Baebius Successus, set up by his son of the same name and his wife Secundina Primus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500102.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vibrius Cilo made this for Vibria Victoria, his deserving fellow slave.
EDCS-72500102.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibria Victoria, set up by her fellow slave Vibrius Cilo, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500103.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caldia Iusta made this for her deserving husband Baebius Felix, with whom she lived for 18 years.
EDCS-72500103.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Baebius Felix, set up by his wife Caldia Iusta, who records their 18 years of marriage, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500104.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Spedius Severianus [made this] for Auguria, with whom he lived for [6] years.
EDCS-72500104.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Auguria, set up by her husband Spedius Severianus, who records their marriage duration, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500106.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caius Caecilius Felix made this for his incomparable, deserving wife Accania Firmina, without complaint, with whom he lived for 23 years.
EDCS-72500106.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Accania Firmina, set up by her husband Caius Caecilius Felix, who emphasizes her incomparable qualities and their 23-year marriage, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500109.translation: To Hercules. Caius Aurellius Probus gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-72500109.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Hercules, dedicated by Caius Aurellius Probus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500111.translation: To Gaius Popillius Secundus, son of Publius, of the Cornelia tribe, quaestor, aedile with jurisdiction, and to his wife Maria Procula, daughter of Sextus.
EDCS-72500111.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Popillius Secundus and his wife Maria Procula, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500229.translation: To the departed spirits. To Aetatia Rhodope, freedwoman of Quintus, mother, most excellent, most sweet. Quintus Aetatius Paulus (dedicated this).
EDCS-72500229.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Quintus Aetatius Paulus to his mother Aetatia Rhodope, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500230.translation: To the departed spirits. To Gaius Albanus Iucundus, son [---].
EDCS-72500230.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Gaius Albanus Iucundus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500231.translation: To the departed spirits. To Gaius Fannius Iovinianus, son of Gaius, [---].
EDCS-72500231.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Gaius Fannius Iovinianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500232.translation: To the departed spirits. Fonteius Chresimus. Fonteius Firmus, father, to his dearest son, made (this).
EDCS-72500232.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Fonteius Firmus for his son Fonteius Chresimus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500236.translation: To the departed spirits. To the most fortunate son, unhappy. Felix and Felicissima, parents (dedicated this).
EDCS-72500236.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from parents Felix and Felicissima for their son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500240.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Quintus Fabius Clytianus, junior, son of Quintus, to his wife Filistia Marcella, daughter of Gaius, made (this) to a well-deserving woman.
EDCS-72500240.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Quintus Fabius Clytianus junior to his wife Filistia Marcella, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500241.translation: To Quinctia Maxmilla, daughter of Marcus, his best wife, and to his dearest daughter. Marcus Socellius Januarius, husband, and Laberia Restituta, mother, daughter of Publius, made (this). She lived 24 years.
EDCS-72500241.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband and mother for Quinctia Maximilla, who died at the age of 24, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72500242.translation: For the memory and the spirits of Valeria Priscilla.
EDCS-72500242.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Priscilla, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72800041.translation: Publius Baius Rufus, son of Lucius, and Marcus Lucius, son of Marcus, [---], the curators of the baths, by decree of the decurions, saw to the making of the bath, the lead pipes, the tub, and the reservoir, and approved them.
EDCS-72800041.summary: This inscription records the construction and approval of public baths, including fixtures and a reservoir, by local magistrates (aediles) in the Roman period.

EDCS-74300193.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary funerary dedication to Antonia Antipatra, likely a woman's name.

EDCS-74300195.translation: Of Lucius Aespicius Eronis, freedman of Lucius. 12 feet in front, 12 feet in depth.
EDCS-74300195.summary: This is a land boundary marker, probably for a tomb, specifying dimensions.

EDCS-74300196.translation: Lucius Mamidius Faustus, son of Lucius, for himself and for his patron Phliodamus, freedman of Lucius. 12 feet in front, 12 feet in depth.
EDCS-74300196.summary: This inscription marks land, likely for a tomb, and names the owner and his patron.

EDCS-74300197.translation: To the divine shades. Helene, the mother, made [this] for her well-deserving son Maximius, who lived for 3 years.
EDCS-74300197.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a mother to her young son.

EDCS-75200374.translation: Marcus Asculeius Rubrianus erected [this] for his father Marcus Asculeius Restitutus, of the Horatia tribe, quaestor, twice aedile, and iudex, and for his mother Rubria Theatice, daughter of Publius, who was well-deserving.
EDCS-75200374.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a son for his deceased father, who held several magistracies, and his mother.

EDCS-75200375.translation: [---]us, son of Lucius. Lucius Pullius [---], son of Lucius. [---] twice iudex.
EDCS-75200375.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription possibly related to a pair of magistrates, one of whom was named Lucius Pullius.

EDCS-75200376.translation: Drussia Symphascusa erected [this] for her husband Quintus Drussius Lido, son of Quintus.
EDCS-75200376.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a wife to her husband.

EDCS-75200377.translation: To the divine shades. Herennius December erected [this] for his well-deserving wife Gavia Tycheni.
EDCS-75200377.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a husband to his wife.

EDCS-75200378.translation: To the divine shades. Neritus, son [of---], and Appuleia Fortunata erected [this] for their most dutiful mother Modesta Appuleia Iusta, her servant, and for themselves.
EDCS-75200378.summary: This is a funerary dedication from children for their mother and themselves, mentioning her status as a servant.

EDCS-75200379.translation: To the divine shades. Quintus Ovius Mercator, who lived 9 years, Quintus Ovius Silvanus, who lived 7 years. Quintus Ovius Ursulus and Silvana, unhappy parents, erected [this] for their sons, as the sons ought to have done for their parents.
EDCS-75200379.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from parents for their young sons, expressing their grief and the reversal of familial roles.

EDCS-75200380.translation: To Publius Babrius Lysimachus, freedman of Publius; to Babria Cinyra, freedwoman of a woman (Babria); to Minatia Prisca, freedwoman of Gaius; to Babria Penthesilea, freedwoman of a woman (Babria); for herself, her brother, her sister, and her cousin, having made this while alive.
EDCS-75200380.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by an individual for herself and her relatives, likely a family tomb.

EDCS-75200382.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Eggia Eutychidis, dearest daughter, who lived...
EDCS-75200382.summary: This is the beginning of a funerary epitaph for a daughter named Eggia Eutychidis.

EDCS-75200384.translation: [...]. To Lucius Cluatius [...]. To Lucius Cluatius [...]. To Cluatia, daughter of Lucius [...]. Tamphia, freedwoman of a woman [...].
EDCS-75200384.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph that names members of the Cluatius and Tamphia families.

EDCS-75500097.translation: Vibidia Fortunata, freedwoman of Sextus, placed this offering to Venus; the place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-75500097.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Venus by a freedwoman, with the land for the monument provided by local authorities.

EDCS-75500098.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Gaius Alarius Firmus, well-deserving husband, (this was) made.
EDCS-75500098.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a spouse for a deserving husband named Gaius Alarius Firmus.

EDCS-75500099.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Alaria Firmina, who lived 8 years and 58 days; her parents made this for their well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-75500099.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl, Alaria Firmina, set up by her parents.

EDCS-75600036.translation: To the Divine Shades. Liburnius Ianuarius lived approximately 50 years; he is buried here. Mecista placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-75600036.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Liburnius Ianuarius, set up by his wife Mecista.

EDCS-75600037.translation: To the Divine Shades. Faustinus, slave of Ennius, lived 23 years; he is buried here. [This was set up] for his well-deserving fellow slave.
EDCS-75600037.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Faustinus, a slave, set up by a fellow slave.

EDCS-75600038.translation: To the Divine Shades. Prima, slave of Severus, lived 10 years; here is her mother [who set this up] for her deserving daughter.
EDCS-75600038.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Prima, a slave, set up by her mother.

EDCS-76000074.translation: [...]. To Posilla, daughter of Lucius [...]. By her will [...].
EDCS-76000074.summary: This is a fragmented inscription indicating a funerary monument established according to the will of the deceased named Posilla.

EDCS-76000080.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Optatus, a freedman.

EDCS-76000081.translation: Obialus, the farm manager of Gnaeus Sentius, lies here.
EDCS-76000081.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Obialus, a farm manager.

EDCS-76000082.translation: Statilius Ianuaris, called Megistio, a freedman, lies here; he lived 7 years.
EDCS-76000082.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial and lifespan of Statilius Ianuaris, a freedman.

EDCS-76000083.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan of Corpenius.

EDCS-76000084.summary: This is a tombstone recording the name and status of Philodamus, a slave.

EDCS-76000087.translation: To the gods, a departed spirit. Symphorus, slave of Annia, lived well for 50 years. Prisca [placed this] for [her] well-deserving [slave].
EDCS-76000087.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to Symphorus by her owner Prisca, recording her lifespan and good conduct.

EDCS-76000091.summary: This is a tombstone fragment that records the name and parentage of Vargunius Bestylus.

EDCS-76000095.translation: Antonia Eunete lived 50 years; she lies here.
EDCS-76000095.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan and burial of Antonia Eunete.

EDCS-76000096.translation: Gaius Maius Vitales lived 50 years; he lies here.
EDCS-76000096.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lifespan and burial of Gaius Maius Vitales.

EDCS-76000098.translation: To the gods, a departed spirit. Hermes, slave of Sisenna, lived 24 years; he lies here. Tryphaera, mother, [placed this].
EDCS-76000098.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to Hermes by his mother Tryphaera, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-76000100.translation: To the shades of the departed. Subisca, servant of Iustus, [---]
EDCS-76000100.summary: A funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld, likely for a freedwoman named Subisca.

EDCS-76000242.summary: A brief inscription recording the name of the scribe, Epictetus.

EDCS-76100011.translation: Mallia Veneria lived for 3 years, buried here.
EDCS-76100011.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Mallia Veneria, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-76100092.translation: Marcus Aurelius Serenus, also known as Heliodorus, Alexandrian, and Elios and Delphos, a councillor, and Lacedaemonian, and Grammateus of the Xystus, lived for 63 years and 11 days. Artemidorus made this. Farewell.
EDCS-76100092.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Serenus, who held multiple civic and official positions, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-76100094.translation: Dionysios, son of Dionysios, Athenian, and Kosmianós, and Seleukos dedicated to the god, to Tarentas.
EDCS-76100094.summary: A dedication to the god Tarentas by three individuals, likely from the Hellenistic or Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76100095.summary: A fragmentary inscription that appears to be a dedication to Tarentas, with the name Sym[---] possibly being the dedicator.

EDCS-76100119.translation: Hermas, freedman of Hermolaos, lived for 50 years. Musa lived for 25 years, buried here.
EDCS-76100119.summary: A bilingual funerary inscription commemorating two individuals, Hermas and Musa, one Greek and the other Latin.

EDCS-76100120.translation: To the Chthonic Gods. Julius Paulus to Proclus, his foster-child, aged 20.
EDCS-76100120.summary: A dedication to the underworld gods by Julius Paulus for his foster-child Proclus.

EDCS-76200043.translation: To the shades of the departed. Gaius Calvius Tirannus lived for 2 years, buried here. Primus, the slave of the son of Laenilla, placed this.
EDCS-76200043.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Calvius Tirannus, set up by a slave for the son of Laenilla.

EDCS-76200046.translation: Gaius Vettius Gal(eria) Bassus, soldier of the 7th Praetorian Cohort, made [this] for Gaius Vettius Aug(ustalis), his father, and Vettia Fortunata, his mother, freedmen and their freedmen.
EDCS-76200046.summary: A funerary monument set up by a soldier of the Praetorian Guard for his parents and their freedmen, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-76200047.translation: To Gnaeus Ennius Modestus Ulpianus, son of Gnaeus. He lived 7 months and 24 days. His mother, Mettia Civitas, made this.
EDCS-76200047.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child, Gnaeus Ennius Modestus Ulpianus, erected by his mother, Mettia Civitas.

EDCS-76200048.translation: To the memory of Pontus Callistus. Gentia Vibiana made this for her well-deserving husband with her son.
EDCS-76200048.summary: A funerary dedication by Gentia Vibiana to her husband, Pontus Callistus, with her son.

EDCS-76200225.translation: A pot belonging to Lucius Aemilius Eutycus.
EDCS-76200225.summary: An inscription on a pot, likely indicating ownership by Lucius Aemilius Eutycus.

EDCS-76300034.translation: His sister made this for the well-deserving Caius Barb[---] for himself. He lived 60 years.
EDCS-76300034.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Barb[---], erected by his sister, recording his age at death.

EDCS-76300036.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Fundanius Velox [erected this for] Gaius Septimius Celer, a soldier of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum. He lived 23 years.
EDCS-76300036.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Septimius Celer, a soldier in the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, erected by Marcus Fundanius Velox.

EDCS-76300037.translation: Sisenes the slave lived 60 years. He lies here. Clara, his wife, [made this for him] as well-deserving.
EDCS-76300037.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sisenes, a slave, erected by his wife Clara, stating his age and place of burial.

EDCS-76300040.summary: A fragmented funerary epitaph that records the age of an individual who lived 10 years.

EDCS-76300044.translation: Salvius Celer, son of Lucius, of the Pol(lia) tribe, veteran of the IV Scythica legion, curator elected for veterans, aedile, quinquennial duovir. He served as a cavalryman for 30 years and lived 55 years. He lies here. Antonia Fortunata, freedwoman of the veteran, lived 60 years. She lies here. Aulus Titinius Italus lived 50 years. He lies here.
EDCS-76300044.summary: A complex funerary inscription for multiple individuals, including Salvius Celer (a veteran with significant public service roles), Antonia Fortunata, and Aulus Titinius Italus, indicating their ages and burial places.

EDCS-76300051.translation: To Aurelia Bassia. Bassianus, her son, made this for [her] as well-deserving.
EDCS-76300051.summary: A funerary dedication by Bassianus to his mother, Aurelia Bassia.

EDCS-76300052.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Betitius Derisor made this for Peregrina, his fellow slave, as well-deserving.
EDCS-76300052.summary: A funerary dedication by Gaius Betitius Derisor to his fellow slave, Peregrina.

EDCS-76300066.translation: To the departed spirits. [---]ta M[---]r[---] lived 20 years [---]. Restutus for his well-deserving slave woman, daughter, son, mother, brother.
EDCS-76300066.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a person identified by fragmented names, likely a slave, with the monument erected by multiple family members.

EDCS-76300067.translation: Saturnina lived 25 years; she lies here.
EDCS-76300067.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Saturnina, indicating her lifespan and burial location.

EDCS-76300068.translation: Papas lived 65 years; he lies here. His son made it.
EDCS-76300068.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Papas, stating his age and that the monument was erected by his son.

EDCS-76300069.translation: Calvia Tirene, daughter of Gaius Calvius Tira, lived 3 years; she lies here.
EDCS-76300069.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Calvia Tirene, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-76300070.translation: Hyle, slave of Iulla Iusta, lived 1 year and 2 months. Fortunatus and Primigenia made it for their most beloved daughter. She lies here. // For the well-deserving.
EDCS-76300070.summary: This funerary inscription records the short life of Hyle, a slave girl, and was made by her parents, Fortunatus and Primigenia.

EDCS-76300071.summary: This inscription appears to be the beginning of a funerary monument or a dedication, mentioning Gaius Calvius Aprilis.

EDCS-76300072.translation: To the departed spirits. Athenodorus, slave of Claudius Paternus, freedman of Gaius, lived 31 years. Euposia, mother, for her sweetest son.
EDCS-76300072.summary: This funerary inscription honors Athenodorus, a slave, and was erected by his mother, Euposia.

EDCS-76300073.translation: Stemon lies here; he lived 40 years.
EDCS-76300073.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Stemon, stating his burial location and lifespan.

EDCS-76400060.summary: This funerary epitaph records the exceptionally long life of Quintus Veturius Malchio.

EDCS-76400077.translation: To the departed spirits. Avilia Vera made [it] for her husband Quintus Firvius Successus, the well-deserving.
EDCS-76400077.summary: This funerary inscription records a wife, Avilia Vera, erecting a monument for her deserving husband, Quintus Firvius Successus.

EDCS-76400155.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 13th time, imperator for the 6th time, consul for the 5th time, father of the fatherland, constructed the road and bridges from Benevento to Brundisium at his own expense.
EDCS-76400155.summary: This is an imperial dedication, likely from the early 2nd century CE, recording Emperor Trajan's construction of a road and bridges.

EDCS-76600315.translation: Helix, slave of Vibidia Elaes, made this for his fellow slave Stabila, who deserved well.
EDCS-76600315.summary: This is a funerary dedication, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE, made by a slave for a fellow slave.

EDCS-76700022.summary: This inscription is too fragmentary to provide further details.

EDCS-76700023.translation: Vibia Tele lived for 35 years and is buried here.
EDCS-76700023.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE, recording the name and age of the deceased.

EDCS-76700024.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE, recording the burial of at least two individuals.

EDCS-76700030.translation: To perpetual memory. For Marcus Bassaeus Onomastus, son of Marcus Bassaeus, my dearest son and most desired for his abundant piety, who for his merits...
EDCS-76700030.summary: This is a funerary dedication, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE, to a deceased son, praising his piety and merits.

EDCS-76800077.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Zosima lived for 35 years and is buried here. Lucius Iulius Valens made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-76800077.summary: This is a funerary epitaph and dedication, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE, for a wife named Iulia Zosima, made by her husband.

EDCS-76800078.translation: ...Aurelius Cesianus lived for 30 years and is buried here.
EDCS-76800078.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE, recording the name and age of the deceased.

EDCS-76800087.translation: ...usa, slave of Perce[nnius], lived for 9 years and is buried here.
EDCS-76800087.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE, for a young slave girl.

EDCS-76800091.translation: ...lived for 20 years and is buried here. ... Bestyll[a(?)], freedwoman(?), mother, made this.
EDCS-76800091.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE, potentially for a freedwoman, with a dedication from her mother.

EDCS-76800093.summary: This is a funerary inscription, possibly for a slave belonging to Mummia, indicating a burial place.

EDCS-76900030.translation: Lived for [3] years. Primus [3]. [3] are here. For mother, son [3]. VIII [3].
EDCS-76900030.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription that records the life span of an individual named Primus and mentions family members, likely a mother and son.

EDCS-76900037.translation: Gaius Vetulen[us...]. Lived for 50 years. Vetulen[...].chio set up.
EDCS-76900037.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Vetulenus, recording his lifespan and mentioning someone named Vetulen[...].chio as the person who erected it.

EDCS-76900044.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record the name Arrun/tius.

EDCS-76900045.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely naming Lucius Co[...] and his freedman O[...].

EDCS-76900048.translation: To the departed spirits. [...]nio Dianensi who lived for 5 years. Ato[...]. [3] EI.
EDCS-76900048.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits for Dianensi, who lived 5 years, with mentions of Ato and other incomplete names.

EDCS-76900050.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record the names Julius Optatus and [...]lia.

EDCS-76900051.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records parts of the names Clodi[...] and Arr[...].

EDCS-76900054.translation: Octavius Celticus, priest. Lived for 30 years. Here lies.
EDCS-76900054.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Octavius Celticus, a priest, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-76900057.translation: Lucius' freedman Fortunatus. Lived for 50 years. Here lies.
EDCS-76900057.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Fortunatus, a freedman of Lucius, who lived for 50 years.

EDCS-76900058.translation: To the departed spirits. Titus Paphrius Eulimenus lived [3] years, is buried here. Baebia Prote lived 60 years, is buried here.
EDCS-76900058.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Paphrius Eulimenus and Baebia Prote, likely a couple, with ages provided.

EDCS-76900059.translation: Lived 15 years, is buried here. [---] Sabellus? made [this] for [his] well-deserving son.
EDCS-76900059.summary: A funerary epitaph for a son, likely named Sabellus, with an indication of his age at death and a parent's dedication.

EDCS-76900060.translation: Valeria Aphrodisia, a freedwoman of the woman, chaste, lived chaste for 41 years, is buried here.
EDCS-76900060.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Aphrodisia, a freedwoman, noting her age and virtuous life.

EDCS-77200275.summary: The beginning of a funerary inscription dedicated to Secunda.

EDCS-77500001.translation: To Deccia Arsinoe, freedwoman of Lucius, 20 feet in the field, 20 feet in the front.
EDCS-77500001.summary: A boundary marker or property description related to Deccia Arsinoe, a freedwoman.

EDCS-77700091.translation: Lived 10 years, Valeri[---]; [---] Valeria Spendusa, freedwoman of Publius, [---]; [---] Nebridi.
EDCS-77700091.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph mentioning a person named Valeri, Valeria Spendusa, and Nebridi, with an age for one individual.

EDCS-77700200.translation: Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, in his 8th consulship, restored the boundaries of the public lands of the municipium of Tarentum according to the Gracchian plan.
EDCS-77700200.summary: An official inscription recording Emperor Vespasian's restoration of land boundaries in Tarentum, dating to 79 CE.

EDCS-78200001.summary: A simple inscription identifying Gennia Urbana, a freedwoman of Gaius.

EDCS-78800043.translation: Aga[---], lived 2[---] years, and Marsi[---] Prima m[---]
EDCS-78800043.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph mentioning individuals named Aga, Marsi, and Prima, with partial age information.

EDCS-80200062.summary: A dedicatory or ownership inscription referring to Publius Luccius Fesus Fortunatus.

EDCS-80900022.translation: To Gaius Vibius Postumus, son of Gaius, former urban praetor, patron.
EDCS-80900022.summary: This is an honorary inscription honoring Gaius Vibius Postumus, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-81100044.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Gaius Calidius Probus (or Metilius Probus?).
EDCS-81100044.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus, made by Gaius Calidius Probus, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-81100045.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Tullia Priscilla, who lived 50 years. Titus Tullius Felix made this for his most innocent mother, well-deserving.
EDCS-81100045.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tullia Priscilla, set up by her son Titus Tullius Felix, likely dating from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-81200025.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Crassicius Felicissimus, their most sweet infant son. Offellius Modestus and Crassicia Felicula, parents, made this for him, well-deserving.
EDCS-81200025.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an infant son, Crassicius Felicissimus, set up by his parents Offellius Modestus and Crassicia Felicula, likely dating from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-81200088.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Hertilia Victoria, most unfortunate girl, incomparable woman, who lived 21 years and 30 and a half days. Aelius Pardus set this up for his wife, with whom he lived two years and three months, well-deserving.
EDCS-81200088.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Hertilia Victoria, set up by her husband Aelius Pardus, likely dating from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-81400086.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Leo. Myrtilus and Leo, sons, made this for their father, well-deserving.
EDCS-81400086.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Leo, set up by his sons Myrtilus and Leo, likely dating from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-81400087.translation: To her son Saturninus. Herculania, mother, made this, well-deserving, who followed her father after 21 days and 1 hour.
EDCS-81400087.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Saturninus, set up by his mother Herculania, likely dating from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-81400088.translation: Set up for good memory. Aurelia Sabbatis made this for her most sweet son, Aurelius Paulus, well-deserving. Renata [was the mother].
EDCS-81400088.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Paulus, set up by his mother Aurelia Sabbatis (Renata), likely dating from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-81400121.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed and eternal peace. For Quintus Tertinius Faustinus, soldier of the 22nd Legion Primigenia Pia Fidelis, a frumentarius. Lucius Moderatius Tertinus, soldier and procurator, saw to the setting up.
EDCS-81400121.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Tertinius Faustinus, a soldier of the 22nd Legion, set up by a fellow soldier and procurator, likely dating from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-81900005.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Sabidia Iusta, a most rare woman.
EDCS-81900005.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sabidia Iusta, likely dating from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-82000094.translation: To Aulus Vesonius, priest, Vesonia Pontia, freedwoman of Aulus, and Statia, freedwoman of a woman, Zosima, dedicated this.
EDCS-82000094.summary: Funerary dedication by freedwomen to their former master and patroness, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE based on prosopography and naming conventions.
